name: Claude Code
model_backend: Claude (Anthropic own models); optionally routed via Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, or Microsoft Azure Foundry
data_handling_note: Consumer tiers (Free/Pro/Max) train on data by default with opt-out. Commercial tiers (Team, Enterprise, API) do NOT train on data per commercial terms. Zero Data Retention (ZDR) is available for Enterprise and API users subject to Anthropic approval - applies to Claude Code accessed via Enterprise plans or API key. ZDR does NOT apply to Pro/Max consumer subscriptions. Free tier is highest-risk.

suitability_notes.personal: Requires Enterprise or API plan (no training on data by default) plus a signed DPA. Zero Data Retention addendum should be requested. Pro/Max consumer plans are not suitable without opt-out.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

pricing: Free (limited) - Pro $20/mo - Max $100-200/mo - Team Premium $150/seat/mo - Enterprise custom; Education/university plan available for institutions (contact sales)
academic: Education plan available for universities (students, faculty, staff) at discounted rates; academic research credit programs available - contact sales@anthropic.com
notes: Terminal CLI remains the primary surface but Claude Code is now a multi-surface product (CLI, VS Code, JetBrains, desktop app, web, iOS). Official docs are now at code.claude.com. Consumer plans train on data by default - researchers must use Enterprise/API + ZDR + DPA for personal or special-category data. No self-hosting or air-gap option exists. HIPAA BAA requires qualifying and contacting sales. ISO/IEC 42001:2023 (AI management systems) is a notable addition alongside ISO 27001.

name: OpenAI Codex
model_backend: GPT-5.x-Codex (GPT-5.4, GPT-5.5; OpenAI proprietary)
data_handling_note: API, ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, and Edu do NOT train on user data by default. API data retained up to 30 days for abuse monitoring then deleted. Zero Data Retention (ZDR) available for eligible enterprise customers on supported endpoints - not self-serve, requires OpenAI account team enablement. Consumer ChatGPT Free/Plus may use data to train unless opted out. EU data residency available for eligible enterprise contracts.

suitability_notes.personal: Use ChatGPT Business/Enterprise or API tier (no training by default) plus sign a DPA; do not use consumer Free/Plus without opting out of data sharing. ZDR available for enterprise via account team.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only, no self-hosting or air-gap option. Even with ZDR and HIPAA BAA (enterprise), data transits OpenAI cloud infrastructure - not suitable for special-category health/genetic data under GDPR.

pricing: Free (limited) - Go $8/mo - Plus $20/mo - Pro 5x $100/mo - Pro 20x $200/mo - Business (pay-as-you-go, usage-based Codex seats from April 2026) - Enterprise/Edu (custom). Students (US/Canada): $100 in Codex credits via SheerID verification.
academic: $100 in Codex credits for verified university students in US and Canada (one-time, expires 12 months). ChatGPT Edu plan for institutions (custom pricing).
notes: The legacy Codex API (2021 code-completion model, codex-mini-latest) was deprecated February 2026 - this record refers to the current Codex AI agent launched April 2025. By March 2026 it had 2M+ weekly active users. Codex CLI version 0.141.0 released June 18 2026. Codex Security (vulnerability scanning) added March 2026. Available on AWS as of June 2026.

name: Gemini CLI
model_backend: Gemini 3.x (Google proprietary)
data_handling_note: Free/consumer tier (Google Account login) collects prompts and code and may train on them - HIGH RISK by default. Paid Gemini API tier and Vertex AI do NOT train on data; inputs treated as confidential. Enterprise (Code Assist Standard/Enterprise) is also confidential, no training. The safe no-train path requires a paid API key or Vertex AI configuration. Vertex AI supports EU data residency (europe-west12, de-central1) but region pinning must be set per-call in code, not at project level.

suitability_notes.personal: Use paid Gemini API key or Vertex AI (no-train) + EU region pinning (Vertex). Free/consumer tier trains on data - not suitable for personal data.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

pricing: Original Gemini CLI: free tier (1,000 req/day with Google Account) or pay-per-token via Gemini API / Vertex AI. As of June 18 2026, free and consumer (AI Pro, Ultra) tiers migrated to Antigravity CLI (closed-source). Enterprise: Code Assist Standard/Enterprise seats. Antigravity CLI pricing not yet fully published.
academic: No dedicated academic/student discount program identified; free tier (now Antigravity) available to any Google Account but carries training-data risk.
notes: The product is in active transition as of the fact-check date (June 20 2026). The original open-source Gemini CLI GitHub repo (Apache 2.0) remains available for self-build but Google has ended backend service for free and consumer tiers. Antigravity CLI is the current product but is proprietary/closed-source, which represents a significant philosophical shift. Enterprise users on Code Assist Standard/Enterprise or Vertex AI retain Gemini model access. For research use, the safest path is Vertex AI with a paid key + EU region configured per call. The GDPR/compliance posture is inherited from Google Cloud / Vertex AI, not from the CLI itself.

name: Aider
model_backend: BYO key - local model (Ollama, etc.) or any cloud API (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, xAI, etc.)
data_handling_note: Aider itself never collects user code, chat messages, or API keys. Analytics are strictly opt-in and cover only anonymous usage stats (no code). When paired with a local model the data never leaves the machine. When paired with a cloud API, privacy follows that provider's policy. No SOC 2, HIPAA, or GDPR certifications exist for Aider itself - compliance is entirely determined by the chosen LLM backend.

suitability_notes.personal: ok when using a local model (data never leaves machine) or a cloud API that provides a DPA and no-train guarantee; cfg if using a consumer cloud API without a DPA.
suitability_notes.special: ok only when paired with a fully local model (e.g. Ollama); with any cloud API, data leaves the machine and no BAA or on-prem path is provided by Aider itself.

pricing: Free (open source, Apache-2.0); user pays their chosen LLM provider directly - Aider charges nothing
academic: free
notes: Pair-programming CLI with 46.5k GitHub stars and 93 releases as of mid-2026. Capability tracks whichever model you connect. Architect mode allows pairing a planner model with a cheaper editor model on the same task. No vendor-provided compliance certifications - researchers must evaluate the chosen LLM backend independently.

name: Goose
vendor: AAIF / Linux Foundation (originally Block; donated to Agentic AI Foundation in Dec 2025)
model_backend: BYO key - 40+ providers: local (Ollama, LM Studio, Docker Model Runner) or cloud (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, Bedrock, etc.)
data_handling_note: Goose itself sends no conversations, code, or personal data to Block or AAIF. With a local model (Ollama, LM Studio, etc.) everything stays fully on-device. Anonymous usage telemetry (OS version, model provider name, token counts, error type only - no code or prompt content) is collected by default but can be disabled via Settings or GOOSE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false. When using cloud LLMs, data is sent to that provider under their own policies. No SOC 2 / HIPAA certifications from the project itself; compliance is inherited from the chosen model provider.

suitability_notes.personal: Use a local model (Ollama/LM Studio) or a cloud provider with a DPA/no-train agreement; disable telemetry (GOOSE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false) before use with personal data.
suitability_notes.special: Must use a fully local model (Ollama etc.) so data never leaves the machine, AND disable telemetry. No BAA or clinical certifications exist at the Goose project level - compliance depends entirely on the self-hosted model provider.

pricing: Free (Apache 2.0 open source; no paid tiers)
academic: free
notes: Goose is both a CLI and a native desktop app (Rust-based, available for macOS/Linux/Windows). As of June 2026 it has v1.38.0, ~50k GitHub stars, and 70+ MCP extensions. The project is now under Linux Foundation/AAIF neutral governance with Block as one of many platinum members. For ELIXIR use: local model path is fully privacy-safe once telemetry is disabled; cloud path requires per-provider DPA review.

name: Cursor
model_backend: frontier models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI - hosted) + BYO key
data_handling_note: Privacy Mode enforces no-train + ZDR agreements with all model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI). Privacy Mode is OFF by default for Hobby/Pro/Pro+ - users must enable it manually (Settings). For Teams, admins can enforce it org-wide; for Enterprise it is ON by default. With Privacy Mode disabled, Cursor may use codebase data, prompts, and editor actions for training. BYO API key bypasses ZDR - data handling then follows the chosen provider's own policy. No HIPAA BAA on any tier.

suitability_notes.personal: Enable Privacy Mode manually (Hobby/Pro/Pro+) or enforce at org level (Teams/Enterprise); Privacy Mode activates ZDR with all providers. DPA available via subprocessors. GDPR compliance confirmed.
suitability_notes.special: No HIPAA BAA on any tier; all code routes through cloud subprocessors (AWS, Azure, GCP, Cloudflare) with no BAA coverage. Privacy Mode protects IP, not regulated health/genetic data. Cloud-only; no self-hosted or air-gapped option.

pricing: Hobby (free) - Pro ($20/mo) - Pro+ ($60/mo) - Ultra ($200/mo) - Teams ($40/user/mo) - Enterprise (custom). ~20% discount on annual billing. Students: 1 year of Pro free via SheerID verification.
academic: 1 year of Pro free for verified university students via SheerID (cursor.com/students).
notes: AI-native editor built on VS Code fork. Privacy Mode is the critical setting: must be manually enabled for individual users (Hobby/Pro/Pro+); org-enforceable on Teams; on by default for Enterprise. No self-hosted or air-gapped deployment path exists - remains cloud-only. No HIPAA BAA on any tier; unsuitable for special-category (health/genetic) data regardless of Privacy Mode. Customer Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) available at Enterprise tier for embeddings and Cloud Agent data. BYO API key use falls outside ZDR coverage.

name: GitHub Copilot
model_backend: multiple frontier models selectable (GPT-5 mini, Claude Sonnet/Opus, and others; hosted by GitHub/Microsoft)
data_handling_note: MAJOR POLICY CHANGE (April 24, 2026): Free, Pro, and Pro+ tiers now use interaction data (inputs, outputs, code snippets, context) for AI training BY DEFAULT - users must actively opt out in account settings. Business and Enterprise tiers remain excluded from training under the GitHub Data Protection Agreement (DPA). The no-train guarantee therefore requires Business or Enterprise. The free/individual tiers are now opt-out (risky by default). Zero-data-retention is not offered; Business/Enterprise have a DPA prohibiting training use without authorization. EU data residency now available for Enterprise (opt-in, +10% cost), covering EU member states and EFTA countries from May 1, 2026.

suitability_notes.personal: Requires Business or Enterprise tier plus DPA - those tiers contractually exclude code/interaction data from training. Free/Pro/Pro+ tiers now train on interaction data by default (opt-out available but not guaranteed by contract). EU data residency (Enterprise, opt-in) satisfies GDPR data-localisation needs.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only; no self-hosting or air-gapped deployment path. No HIPAA BAA confirmed for GitHub Copilot specifically. Even with Business/Enterprise and EU residency, data leaves the researcher's systems and special-category health/genetic data should not be processed.

pricing: Free - Pro ($10/mo) - Pro+ ($39/mo) - Max ($100/mo) - Business ($19/seat/mo) - Enterprise ($39/user/mo). From June 1 2026, paid plans include monthly GitHub AI Credits; completions are free on all paid plans but chat/agent mode draws from credits.
academic: Free student plan (GitHub Student Developer Pack) available for verified students - reduced feature set vs Pro since March 2026; verified teachers get free Copilot Pro.
notes: The April 24 2026 training policy change is the most critical fact for research/ELIXIR context: researchers using free or personal Pro/Pro+ accounts are now contributing interaction data to model training by default. Only Business and Enterprise have a contractual prohibition on training use. EU data residency (opt-in, Enterprise) now available and aligned to Microsoft EU Data Boundary. No confirmed HIPAA BAA for GitHub Copilot specifically.

name: Windsurf
model_backend: Proprietary SWE-1.6 (default) + frontier models (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini on Pro/Max)
data_handling_note: The current Cognition privacy policy (updated 2026-03-09) states user content CAN be used to train/fine-tune models under the default Terms - training is NOT off by default on the Free/Pro tiers. Zero Data Retention (ZDR) and confirmed no-training is available on Teams/Enterprise only. Enterprise also offers hybrid or fully self-hosted (VPC/on-prem) deployment where code never leaves the customer environment. SOC 2 Type II certified; FedRAMP High ATO available; HIPAA BAAs offered for enterprise. The best available option is therefore "no-train" but it requires the Teams or Enterprise tier to be guaranteed.

suitability_notes.personal: Requires Teams or Enterprise tier to enable Zero Data Retention + DPA; default Free/Pro tiers permit training on user content under current Cognition privacy policy.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-first: the AI runs in the vendor cloud by default (no-train). Only an enterprise on-prem / self-hosted deployment on infrastructure you control could suit special-category data — and only with DPO/IRB approval; the default cloud product is not suitable.

compliance: FedRAMP High (via Palantir FedStart / AWS GovCloud)
GDPR (SCCs / UK IDTA for international transfers)
pricing: Free ($0) - Pro ($20/mo) - Max ($200/mo) - Teams ($80/mo base + $40/seat) - Enterprise (custom). Student discount ~50%+ off Pro with .edu email.
academic: ~50% student discount on Pro tier for verified .edu email holders.
notes: The product is in rapid flux post-acquisition. windsurf.com 308-redirects to devin.ai/desktop; docs.windsurf.com 307-redirects to docs.devin.ai/desktop. The privacy policy data controller is now "Cognition AI, Inc" and explicitly permits training on user content by default - a significant regression from Codeium-era no-train claims. Researchers handling personal or special-category data MUST use the Enterprise tier with self-hosted/VPC deployment and execute a DPA. The proprietary SWE-1.6 model delivers near-frontier performance; frontier models (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini) are accessible on Pro and above.

name: Google Antigravity
model_backend: Gemini 3.1 Pro / 3.5 Flash (default); also supports Claude Sonnet/Opus and OpenAI GPT-OSS models
data_handling_note: Free, Pro, Ultra, and Ultra Max consumer tiers use interactions for training by default; users can opt out via settings, but Google has not confirmed that disabling telemetry fully prevents training use. True no-train protection (Google Cloud CDPA / AI/ML Privacy Commitment) requires accessing Antigravity via a Google Cloud Project (GCP) - currently restricted to an invited-team limited preview - or via Google Workspace enterprise (not yet supported as of mid-2026). Default consumer tiers are risky for any non-public data.

suitability_notes.personal: Consumer tiers train on data by default; opt-out is ambiguous. No-train path (GCP enterprise) is in limited preview only - not generally available. Cannot recommend for GDPR-covered personal data until GCP enterprise path opens broadly.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only, no self-hosting or on-premises option. No clinical use / HIPAA-BAA path documented. Not suitable for health, genetic, or clinical data.

compliance: GDPR (via Google Cloud CDPA, GCP enterprise path only)
Google Cloud AI/ML Privacy Commitment (GCP path only)
pricing: Free (rate-limited, all models) - AI Pro $20/mo - AI Ultra $100/mo - AI Ultra Max $200/mo (reduced from $249.99 at I/O 2026) - Pay-as-you-go $25/2,500 credits
academic: No current academic pricing. A 12-month free AI Pro student promotion closed globally by April 2026. Students may use the free tier or informal cost-sharing (family plan ~$3.33/person/month).
notes: Product is in public preview (v2.0.1, May 2026). The enterprise/GCP no-train path exists in principle (Google Cloud CDPA covers it) but Antigravity access via GCP is still invite-only as of mid-2026. Data terms are actively contested in developer forums. Re-verify data handling before any institutional adoption. The antigravity.google domain exists but its terms and pricing pages returned empty content during fetch - cited sources are from Google Developers Blog, the Google AI Developers Forum, and Google Cloud documentation.

name: JetBrains Junie
model_backend: frontier models via JetBrains subscription (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI); BYOK (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI, OpenRouter, GitHub Copilot); local runtimes (Ollama, LM Studio, LiteLLM) via Junie CLI
data_handling_note: JetBrains contractually commits not to train on user inputs/outputs; AI subcontractors bound by same restriction. By default, code is sent to LLM providers without being stored on JetBrains servers (detailed data collection is opt-in only, disabled by default for orgs). 1-year retention applies only if org admin explicitly enables detailed collection. BYOK routes directly to provider, bypassing JetBrains servers. Local model option (Ollama/LM Studio via Junie CLI) means data can stay fully on-device. DPA available at jetbrains.com/legal/dpa/. Data stored in EEA (AWS/GCP EU). Free/individual tier has the same no-train guarantee but no contractual DPA by default.

suitability_notes.personal: Sign DPA (available at jetbrains.com/legal/dpa/) and use AI Enterprise tier; ensure detailed data collection is disabled (default for orgs). BYOK mode eliminates JetBrains data exposure.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only for AI processing. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control; confirm with DPO/IRB.

pricing: AI Free (3 credits/30 days, no cost); AI Pro ($10/mo individual, $20/mo org); AI Ultimate ($30/mo individual, $60/mo org); AI Enterprise (custom via JetBrains IDE Services). BYOK users pay provider rates with no JetBrains markup.
academic: Students and teachers get JetBrains IDEs free (Student Pack); AI Free tier included with educational license; AI Pro/Ultimate not included - students must pay separately. 40% graduation discount available for 2 years post-study.
notes: Junie is a full agentic coding assistant (not just autocomplete) built into all JetBrains IDEs plus a standalone CLI. GA release June 17 2026 added agentic debugging via real IDE debugger, ACP-based IDE integration, async remote task monitoring, and local model support. The local runtime path (Ollama/LM Studio via Junie CLI) is the key unlock for special-category data use. JetBrains Trust Center is at trust-center.jetbrains.com; SOC 2 Type II confirmed, ISO 27001 not confirmed in available sources.

name: Continue.dev
vendor: Continue Dev, Inc. (acquired by Cursor / Anysphere)
model_backend: BYO key - local model (Ollama etc.) or any cloud API; 100+ provider integrations
data_handling_note: Final release v2.0.0 (June 19, 2026) explicitly removed anonymous telemetry. With a local model (Ollama etc.) no data leaves the machine. Cloud agents send code to the chosen LLM provider. The privacy policy (last updated Feb 5, 2026) collected IP/usage analytics from the open-source extension unless opted out; telemetry opt-out was available. No SOC 2, GDPR DPA, HIPAA-BAA, or zero-data-retention terms were documented. The repo is now read-only/frozen; no active vendor to offer compliance agreements. Self-hosting the frozen codebase remains viable for air-gapped use.

suitability_notes.personal: Pair with a local model (Ollama) so code never leaves the machine; avoid cloud-API backends without verifying the provider's DPA. No vendor DPA is available from Continue Dev post-acquisition.
suitability_notes.special: Only viable via fully local model on an air-gapped or self-hosted setup (Apache 2.0 allows this). No vendor BAA or clinical compliance certification exists. Community fork may be needed for long-term maintenance.

pricing: Free (Apache 2.0 open source, final v2.0.0). Prior paid tiers (Solo free / Team $10/dev/month / Enterprise custom) are no longer sold - pricing page redirects to acquisition notice. LLM costs borne by user via chosen provider.
academic: free
notes: The extension, CLI, and JetBrains plugin remain usable from VS Code Marketplace / npm / JetBrains Marketplace. Future bug-fixes and model integrations depend on community forks. For sensitive research data, pair exclusively with a local model; the frozen codebase (Apache 2.0) supports air-gapped self-hosting. Cursor itself is in the process of being acquired by SpaceX ($60B deal announced June 16, 2026), adding further vendor-continuity uncertainty.

name: Cline
model_backend: BYO key - Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, AWS Bedrock, Azure, or local model via Ollama/LM Studio (30+ providers)
data_handling_note: With BYOK (own API keys) Cline itself never receives or stores code/prompts - data goes only to your chosen provider. Free tier ToS grants Cline broad content license, but this does not apply on paid per-seat subscriptions. Enterprise tier explicitly guarantees no data exfiltration and no training on code. With a fully local model (Ollama/LM Studio), data never leaves the device. No SOC 2, HIPAA, or GDPR certifications are publicly listed. Telemetry is opt-in and never includes code or file content.

suitability_notes.personal: Use BYOK with a provider that offers no-training/DPA (e.g. Anthropic API with data-privacy add-on, or Azure OpenAI), or run a local model. Free tier with cloud keys risks broad Cline content license; Enterprise tier removes this risk.
suitability_notes.special: Run with a fully local model (Ollama/LM Studio) so data never leaves the device. Enterprise self-hosted path also provides no-exfiltration guarantee but compliance certs (HIPAA-BAA) are not publicly confirmed - verify with Cline sales before use with health/genetic data.

pricing: Free (open-source, BYOK usage-based, no subscription); Enterprise: custom pricing (contact sales). No academic/student discount listed.
academic: free (open-source tier)
notes: 63.6k GitHub stars; 30+ model providers supported. Enterprise plan adds SSO, RBAC, centralized billing, and guarantees no code exfiltration. With a fully local model the tool is air-gapped by design. The draft listed vendor as "open-source" - now correctly Cline Bot Inc. The draft listed personal/special as ok - corrected to cfg given free-tier ToS content license and absence of confirmed HIPAA-BAA.

name: Tabnine
model_backend: own models + BYO LLM endpoint; air-gapped / on-prem option
data_handling_note: No-train-no-retain policy applies across all paid tiers: code is ephemerally processed for inference then immediately discarded; never stored or used for training. On-prem and air-gapped deployments (zero outbound calls) available at both the $39 Code Assistant and $59 Agentic tiers. There is no free tier since April 2025. SaaS default is US-hosted (AWS North Virginia / GCP Iowa); EU data residency not explicitly documented.

suitability_notes.personal: No-train-no-retain policy applies at all paid tiers; on-prem deployment removes any SaaS data-transit risk. SaaS tier adequate with DPA in place.
suitability_notes.special: Use the on-prem or air-gapped deployment option (available at both paid tiers): code never leaves your network and inference happens on your hardware. Vendor lists HIPAA (via AWS/GCP infra) and ISO 27001/27017/27018 compliance.

pricing: Code Assistant $39/user/month - Agentic Platform $59/user/month (both annual only; free tier discontinued April 2025; 14-day trial available)
academic: None - no academic or student discount listed
notes: Tabnine pivoted to enterprise-only in 2025 and positions itself as the privacy-first alternative to GitHub Copilot. Both paid tiers include on-prem/air-gapped deployment, making it suitable for regulated research environments. EU data residency is not explicitly documented; contact vendor if EU-only data location is required for GDPR purposes. HIPAA/FedRAMP certifications are inherited via AWS/GCP cloud infrastructure, not independently held by Tabnine.

name: Tabby
model_backend: self-hosted open-weights models (Community/Team self-host); BYOK for Pochi agent (cloud)
data_handling_note: Self-hosted Community/Team plans: code never leaves your infrastructure - fully local. Pochi agent (cloud tier) uses a BYOK model (you provide your own LLM API key); cloud billing/token usage handled by TabbyML. Privacy policy mentions data transfer to the US and China for cloud services, and analytics via PostHog. No SOC 2, HIPAA-BAA, or ISO 27001 certifications found. The self-hosted path is air-gappable and remains the safest option for sensitive data.

suitability_notes.personal: Safe when self-hosted (Community or Team tier on your own infrastructure) - data stays on-prem. Cloud tier (Tabby Cloud / Pochi) is not recommended for personal data without a DPA; privacy policy allows transfer to US and China.
suitability_notes.special: Self-hosted deployment is air-gappable and data never leaves your infrastructure, making it suitable for special-category data in principle. However, no vendor-side HIPAA-BAA or formal clinical compliance exists. Configure self-hosted (cfg) and ensure your own infrastructure compliance.

compliance: GDPR (rights acknowledged in privacy policy - but data may transfer to US/China for cloud)
pricing: Free (Community - self-hosted, up to 5 users, Apache 2.0 core) - Team $19/seat/month (up to 50 users, flexible deployment) - Enterprise: custom pricing (unlimited users, customised deployment) - Tabby Cloud Pochi agent: usage-based, $20 free monthly credits; tab completion always free
academic: free (Community self-hosted tier is free with no user cap restriction documented beyond 5-user Community limit; no explicit academic discount program found)
notes: Tabby is actively maintained (21.6k GitHub stars, v0.32.0 released January 2026). Core code completion is Apache 2.0; enterprise features (SSO, LDAP, advanced team management) are under a proprietary EE license. The Pochi agentic feature (launched July 2025) is cloud-only with BYOK LLM keys and usage-based billing. For ELIXIR/research use with sensitive data, the self-hosted Community or Team plan on researcher-controlled infrastructure is the appropriate path; avoid Tabby Cloud for personal or special-category data.

name: Devin
model_backend: frontier models (hosted by Cognition; Pro/Max users can choose OpenAI, Claude, Gemini)
data_handling_note: Docs state "Cognition does not train its models on customer data or code" by default for all plans. However the main privacy policy for individual/consumer accounts mentions using User Content to "train, fine-tune and improve models" - the no-train guarantee is explicit and firm only at Enterprise tier. Free/Pro/Team users should verify their current plan terms. Enterprise adds VPC deployment (data stays in customer tenant). SOC 2 Type II certified. No HIPAA, ISO 27001, FedRAMP documented. DPA link (cognition.ai/legal/data-processing-addendum) returned 404 at time of check - contact vendor to confirm current DPA availability.

suitability_notes.personal: Enterprise tier with explicit no-train commitment and DPA required; consumer/free tiers may allow training on content per privacy policy.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-first: the AI runs in the vendor cloud by default (no-train). Only an enterprise on-prem / self-hosted deployment on infrastructure you control could suit special-category data — and only with DPO/IRB approval; the default cloud product is not suitable.

pricing: Devin Cloud (autonomous agent): Core pay-as-you-go ~$20 base at $2.25/ACU; Team $500/month (250 ACUs included, $2.00/ACU overage); Enterprise custom. Devin Desktop (IDE, formerly Windsurf): Free $0; Pro $20/month; Max $200/month; Teams $80/month base + $40/seat; Enterprise custom. ACU = ~15 min of active agent compute.
academic: No academic or student discount program documented.
notes: Devin is a fully autonomous cloud software engineer (Devin Cloud) and also an IDE/agent manager (Devin Desktop, formerly Windsurf). Enterprise tier includes VPC deployment. The privacy policy redirect from cognition.ai to cognition.com indicates recent domain consolidation. The DPA at the draft URL (cognition.ai/legal/data-processing-addendum) now 404s - vendors should be contacted for current DPA. No HIPAA-BAA, ISO 27001, or FedRAMP certifications found. Major enterprise customers include Goldman Sachs, Citi, Mercedes-Benz, and US Army/Navy.

name: Replit Agent
model_backend: Anthropic Claude (Sonnet 4.5 / Opus 4.5 as of Nov 2025); also Google Gemini 3 (Design Mode); 300+ models via OpenRouter available in AI Integrations
data_handling_note: Commercial/enterprise customers: explicit no-train clause - Replit will not use Customer Content to train ML models (Commercial Agreement). Free/Core (consumer) tier: privacy policy is ambiguous - may use data "to improve machine learning technologies." Default free/Core tier should be treated as risky (opt-out at best). Enterprise plan adds DPA (GDPR-compliant SCCs), SSO/SCIM, and enterprise privacy controls. No HIPAA BAA offered; not suitable for PHI. SOC 2 Type II certified (2025, zero exceptions). Data primarily hosted in US (may include India). No EU data residency guarantee. No self-hosting or on-premises option.

suitability_notes.personal: Personal/pseudonymised data requires a commercial or enterprise plan with the explicit no-train clause (Commercial Agreement) plus a signed DPA. Free and Core tiers are ambiguous on training and should be avoided for GDPR-regulated data.
suitability_notes.special: Not appropriate. Replit does not offer a HIPAA BAA, has no on-premises/self-hosted deployment option, and data is hosted in the US. Health, genetic, or clinical data must not be processed on this platform.

compliance: GDPR (DPA available for EEA/UK/CH enterprise customers, SCCs)
pricing: Free (Starter, limited Agent credits, 1 published project) - Core $25/mo ($20/mo annual, $25 credits, 2 parallel agents) - Pro $100/mo ($95/mo annual, $100 credits, 10 parallel agents) - Enterprise (custom). Effort-based credit pricing for Agent tasks since mid-2025.
academic: No dedicated academic discount listed on pricing page; annual billing saves 5-20%. No student plan identified.
notes: Agent v2 launched Feb 2025 (Claude 3.7 Sonnet); Agent 3 (Sep 2025) added self-testing and up to 200 min autonomous work; upgraded to Claude Sonnet/Opus 4.5 in Nov 2025. Agent 4 (multi-agent canvas) in development. Platform listed on GCP and Azure Marketplaces. Enterprise adds SSO/SAML, SCIM, Security Center (CVE detection, SBOM exports). No self-hosted or air-gapped option exists. Data primarily US-hosted; no EU residency guarantee even under DPA.

name: OpenHands
model_backend: BYO key (any OpenAI-compatible API) or at-cost via OpenHands LLM provider; local models supported
data_handling_note: CRITICAL: The cloud privacy policy (updated Sep 2025) explicitly states All Hands AI MAY use content and feedback to train AI models. Cloud individual tier (free, 10 conv/day) is therefore risky for any non-public data. Self-hosted open-source (MIT) or enterprise VPC deployment (Kubernetes Helm chart, source-available Polyform license) keeps all code and conversations on your own infrastructure with nothing leaving your environment - that is the safe path. No SOC2, HIPAA-BAA, or public DPA found.

suitability_notes.personal: Cloud tier trains on user data by default - do NOT use for personal/pseudonymised data on cloud. Self-host open-source (local Docker/VM) or enterprise VPC and point at a local or no-train LLM API.
suitability_notes.special: Self-host only: deploy open-source locally with a local model so data never leaves your infrastructure. Enterprise VPC also acceptable if verified no-train LLM endpoint is used. Cloud tier is not suitable.

compliance: self-hosted-only (no SOC2/HIPAA-BAA/DPA found for cloud tier)
pricing: Free (open-source, local, unlimited) - Individual cloud: free, 10 conversations/day, BYOK or at-cost tokens - Enterprise: custom pricing, self-hosted VPC via Kubernetes
academic: no explicit academic discount; open-source tier is free
notes: Formerly OpenDevin. Active project with 77.8k GitHub stars and version 1.8.0 (June 10, 2026). Open-source core is MIT-licensed. Cloud tier is free but trains on user data per privacy policy - unsuitable for GDPR-sensitive or special-category data. Enterprise tier offers VPC self-hosting with full data isolation. No public compliance certifications (SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR) found; a Trust Center (Vanta) is linked but not publicly accessible. Enterprise Automations launched May 2026. Works with any OpenAI-compatible endpoint including local models (Ollama, LM Studio, etc.).

name: Jupyter AI
model_backend: BYO key (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Mistral, GitHub Copilot, etc.) via ACP; or local model via Ollama, GPT4All, vLLM; v3 uses LiteLLM for 1000+ models
data_handling_note: When a local model (Ollama, GPT4All, vLLM) is used, notebook data never leaves the machine - this is the safest configuration. With a BYO cloud API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) the chat interface explicitly sends data to the third-party provider; Jupyter AI documentation warns users to review each provider's privacy policy before sending confidential or sensitive data. Project Jupyter itself collects no telemetry by default. There is no SaaS tier and no cloud service operated by Project Jupyter - all data handling is determined by the user's chosen model backend.

suitability_notes.personal: Use a local model (Ollama, GPT4All, vLLM) - data stays on the machine and never reaches third-party providers. With a cloud API key, the chosen provider's data-use terms govern, which may be incompatible with GDPR obligations.
suitability_notes.special: Use a local model only - special-category (health/genetic/clinical) data must not be sent to cloud API endpoints. Local deployment via Ollama or GPT4All keeps data fully on-premise with no external transmission.

pricing: Free (open source, MIT/BSD licensed); users pay only their own cloud provider API costs if using a cloud backend
academic: free
notes: v3.0.0 (April 2026) is a major architectural shift. The tool now connects AI agents via the Agent Client Protocol - users bring their own agent (Claude Code, Kiro, Goose, OpenCode, etc.) rather than configuring raw API keys directly (though the Jupyternaut persona still uses LiteLLM for direct API access). Agents request human approval before writing files or running commands. For ELIXIR researchers: local Ollama or GPT4All deployment is the recommended path for any sensitive data; cloud backends require reviewing each provider's terms separately. No SaaS component from Project Jupyter - privacy risk is entirely determined by the chosen model/agent backend.

name: Julius AI
model_backend: frontier models (GPT-4/GPT-5, Claude Opus, Gemini; hosted by Julius)
data_handling_note: Julius explicitly states user data is never used to train models internally or externally, and AI providers are contractually bound by this - this applies across all tiers including Free. Data is stored in the US. Enterprise tier adds private VPC deployment and a Data Processing Addendum (DPA) with standard contractual clauses. No HIPAA BAA is explicitly confirmed in public docs; enterprise is the path for regulated industries. Default free/paid cloud tiers: no training, but data stored on Julius servers (not zero-retention).

suitability_notes.personal: No-train policy confirmed across all tiers; however, data is uploaded to and stored on Julius cloud servers in the US. For GDPR personal data, require a signed DPA (available at Enterprise tier). On lower tiers without a DPA, treat as 'no'.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

pricing: Free (15 msg/mo) - Plus $35/mo - Pro $45/mo - Max $200/mo - Ultra $500/mo - Business $375-450/mo (teams) - Enterprise (custom). Annual billing saves ~15-20%.
academic: 50% discount for students and educators on all plans
notes: Julius AI's no-train guarantee is stated publicly and applies to all tiers, but direct access to the privacy policy and security pages was blocked (HTTP 403); claims are corroborated by multiple third-party reviews and Julius's own articles. No confirmed HIPAA BAA in public docs - Enterprise with private VPC is the path for regulated data but should be verified with Julius directly before use. All data processed and stored in the US; no EU data residency option found. Trust center is at trust.julius.ai.

name: Hex Magic
model_backend: OpenAI and Anthropic (hosted; BYOK on Enterprise)
data_handling_note: LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic) operate under zero-data-retention by default and do not train on customer data. Hex itself may use AI session data to improve its own features - admins can opt out without disabling AI entirely. BYOK (OpenAI/Anthropic) is Enterprise-only. EU multi-tenant stack available. HIPAA BAA available on HIPAA multi-tenant and single-tenant tiers (not free/Community/Professional). Free/Community tier carries the same no-train guarantee but lacks enterprise governance controls, so treat it as higher-risk for sensitive data.

suitability_notes.personal: No-train by default across all tiers, but DPA requires a formal agreement (Hex will enter DPAs with customers on request). For GDPR personal data, use the EU multi-tenant stack (Team or Enterprise) and execute a DPA with Hex. Free/Community tier: no DPA path, treat as unsuitable for personal data.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

pricing: Community (free, up to 5 notebooks) - Professional ($36/editor/month) - Team ($75/editor/month) - Enterprise (custom). Academic: Professional plan free for students and educators at universities/bootcamps (contact [email protected]). Non-profits also qualify for free access.
academic: Professional plan free for students and educators (email [email protected]); non-profits also eligible.
notes: Hex Magic is the AI assistant embedded in Hex's notebook/warehouse-native collaborative data platform. No true on-prem or air-gap option - single-tenant VPCs are fully AWS-managed by Hex. Sensitive database schema and code is passed to LLM providers as context even in zero-retention mode, so data minimisation practices are important. ISO 27001 and ISO 27701 are mentioned in Hex's aspirational compliance language but a confirmed, audited certification was not verifiable from public pages at time of review.

name: Kiro
model_backend: Amazon Bedrock (Anthropic Claude Opus/Sonnet/Haiku 4.x variants, DeepSeek v3.2, MiniMax M2.5, GLM-5, Kimi, Qwen; "Auto" mode selects dynamically)
data_handling_note: Free tier and individual (non-enterprise) subscribers: content MAY be used for service improvement including model training; inputs stored up to 60 days. Pro/Pro+/Pro Max/Power (individual, via social/Builder ID): no foundation model training but content storage still occurs. Enterprise (via AWS IAM Identity Center or external IdP): data is NOT stored and NOT used for service improvement; customer-managed KMS keys available. Amazon Q Developer Pro users migrating to Kiro retain no-training protections by logging in with the same credentials. Default free tier IS risky for any sensitive data.

suitability_notes.personal: Personal/pseudonymised data requires Enterprise tier (AWS IAM Identity Center auth) where data is not stored and not used for training. Individual paid tiers (Pro/Pro+/Pro Max/Power) do not train on data but do store content - insufficient for GDPR-sensitive personal data without a DPA. No self-hosting or air-gap option exists.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only for AI processing. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

pricing: Free: 50 credits/month (Claude Sonnet 4.5 + open-weight models). Pro: $19-20/month, 1,000 credits. Pro+: $40/month, 2,000 credits. Pro Max: $100/month, 5,000 credits. Power: $200/month, 10,000 credits. Overages: $0.04/credit across paid tiers. Team/Enterprise: mirrors individual pricing with centralized billing, SSO, and governance. GovCloud pricing ~20% higher. New subscribers get $20 credit on first upgrade.
academic: Student program: eligible university students (limited list including CMU, Georgia Tech, NYU, U Toronto, U Waterloo, UT Austin, U Chicago, and others) get 1,000 credits/month free for one year via SheerID verification; no credit card required. Startup program: up to one year of free Pro+ access. No general researcher or academic institutional discount documented.
use_cases: Spec-driven agentic software development (requirements.md -> design.md -> tasks.md -> code)
AWS-native development (Lambda, ECS, Bedrock, CloudFormation, CDK)
Multi-tool integration (Figma, Postman, Datadog, Terraform, CI/CD)
Parallel agent workflows across large codebases
notes: The IDE runs locally (VS Code / Code OSS), but all AI inference is cloud-based via Amazon Bedrock - there is no local/offline inference mode. "runs_locally" reflects the IDE client running on the developer's machine, not local model inference. EU data residency: GA models use regional cross-region inference (EU-only for EU-region deployments), but experimental/newer models may route globally. No DPA or GDPR-specific documentation was found on kiro.dev as of June 2026; GDPR compliance likely relies on AWS's broader DPA framework. SOC 2 and ISO 27001 not explicitly listed for Kiro (only HIPAA confirmed). Mobile iOS app is in early access.

name: Zed
model_backend: Claude (default, via Anthropic), GPT-4/Codex, Gemini, Amazon Bedrock, xAI Grok, Ollama (local), BYO API key for any provider, Zeta (open-weight edit-prediction model, self-hostable)
data_handling_note: No training on user data by default across all tiers - codified in Terms of Service effective March 2, 2026. Zero data retention agreements with Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, and xAI for Zed-hosted models, with one exception: Anthropic Mythos-class models (e.g. Claude Fable 5) retain prompts/outputs for 30 days for safety review (not training), and this applies even with BYO Anthropic API key. Free/Personal tier is not risky for training but the Anthropic safety-retention exception applies to any tier. Business tier adds org-wide admin controls to lock data-sharing settings off by default and prevent members from opting in. DPA available upon request. BYO key or local Ollama routes data under the respective provider's or local server's terms. SOC 2 Type 1 certification in progress (not yet issued as of mid-2026).

suitability_notes.personal: Personal (pseudonymised, GDPR-scope) data is ok only with BYO API key using a provider with a suitable DPA, or with fully local Ollama models. With Zed-hosted models, data is processed in the US with DPA available on request - this needs to be in place and a GDPR-compliant provider selected. Default free/Pro tiers send data to US servers; configure BYO key + DPA or use local Ollama to reach 'ok' status.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only for AI processing. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control; confirm with DPO/IRB.

compliance: GDPR (privacy policy covers EEA/UK rights, DPA on request, Standard Contractual Clauses for data transfers)
SOC 2 Type 1 (in progress, not yet certified as of June 2026)
pricing: Personal (free): 2,000 accepted edit predictions, unlimited BYO-key usage, no hosted AI model access. Pro ($10/month, was $20 before May 6 2026): unlimited edit predictions, $5/month token credits included, usage-based billing at API list price +10% beyond credits, two-week free trial. Business ($30/seat/month): adds org-wide AI model policies, Data & Privacy admin dashboard, role-based access controls, unified spend visibility, lock data-sharing settings org-wide; enterprise contracts available at 25+ seats via order form. No academic or student discounts listed.
academic: No academic or student discount program listed on the pricing page as of June 2026. The free Personal tier is fully functional for researchers using BYO API keys and local models.
use_cases: AI-assisted code editing with agentic multi-file editing
Parallel agent threads for simultaneous tasks across a project
Local/private coding with Ollama integration (data stays on machine)
BYO API key setup for privacy-conscious researchers
Edit prediction via open-weight Zeta model (self-hostable)
MCP server integration for extended tool access
Terminal Threads for running Claude Code or Amp as sidebar agents
notes: Zed is especially relevant for privacy-conscious researchers because: (1) the Personal tier is free and supports BYO API keys with no Zed data involvement; (2) Ollama integration enables fully local inference with zero data egress; (3) the open-weight Zeta model can be self-hosted for edit predictions; (4) the no-training commitment is now contractually binding (March 2026 ToS). The main caveats are that Anthropic Mythos-class models (including Claude Fable 5) trigger a 30-day safety retention regardless of key type, SOC 2 is still pending, no HIPAA BAA is offered, and PHI is contractually prohibited on hosted endpoints. For ELIXIR researchers: nonsensitive code is ok on any tier; GDPR-scope data needs BYO key + DPA or local mode; clinical/genomic data should use local-only Ollama configuration.

name: Amp
vendor: Amp Frontier Corporation (spun out of Sourcegraph December 2025)
model_backend: Claude Opus 4.8 (smart mode), GPT-5.5 (rush/deep modes); always uses latest frontier models - backend is non-configurable, managed by Amp
data_handling_note: Zero Data Retention (ZDR) is available only on the Enterprise plan. Standard and free-tier users may have inputs retained for service purposes; the terms state Amp may retain user content "for the sole purpose of providing the Services." The free tier no longer requires opting in to data training (changed Oct 2025), but ZDR is not guaranteed outside Enterprise. Data is stored in the US (Google Cloud Platform). LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) may cache inputs up to 24 hours even under Enterprise ZDR. No self-hosted or on-prem option exists.

suitability_notes.personal: Personal (GDPR-scope) data requires Enterprise plan to unlock Zero Data Retention and SSO/audit logging; GDPR SCCs cover EEA data transfers but data is processed in the US. A DPA-like arrangement is implicit via Enterprise terms but no explicit public DPA document was found on ampcode.com. Standard/free tier should be avoided for pseudonymised personal data.
suitability_notes.special: No self-hosted or air-gapped deployment option exists; the service is cloud-only with data processed in US. No HIPAA BAA is offered. Special-category (health/genetic/clinical) data must not be processed through Amp in any tier. The terms explicitly warn users not to submit 'Sensitive Data' including health information.

compliance: GDPR (SCCs for EEA transfers, rights obligations in privacy policy)
pricing: Pay-as-you-go with zero markup on LLM API costs for individual/team users. Minimum credit purchase: $5. Unused credits expire after 1 year of inactivity. Free tier: ~$10/day in credits (replenished hourly), ad-supported initially then ads removed March 2026 - still offered as daily free allowance. Enterprise: +50% cost over individual pricing; unlocks ZDR, SSO/SCIM, audit logging, MCP allowlists, managed settings. Enterprise onboarding starts with a $1,000 minimum purchase.
academic: No dedicated academic or student discount program found. The free tier ($10/day credits, no payment required) is available to all users including researchers. No evidence of institutional or academic pricing.
use_cases: Agentic software development across entire codebases
Multi-platform agent management (CLI started, monitored via web or mobile)
MCP server integration for custom tooling
Long-running autonomous coding tasks with context compaction
notes: The "Neo" mentioned in the lead as a rebrand was actually a temporary suffix for the rebuilt CLI architecture, not a product rename. As of May 27, 2026, it reverted to plain "Amp." The Security Reference page (ampcode.com/security) is the authoritative source for compliance claims. No public DPA document was found on ampcode.com - Enterprise customers likely negotiate terms directly. Amp positions itself as a frontier coding agent, not a data analysis tool; it has no data-science-specific features.

name: Sourcegraph Cody
model_backend: Anthropic Claude (Opus 4.8, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5 + Thinking variants), OpenAI GPT-5/GPT-4o/o3/o4-mini, Google Gemini 2.5/3 series, DeepSeek V2 Lite Base (autocomplete); BYOK/BYOLLM supported on Enterprise
data_handling_note: Zero data retention (ZDR) is guaranteed on Enterprise: partner LLMs discard prompts and outputs immediately after generating a response, and Sourcegraph itself does not use code to train models. On the discontinued free/pro tiers, prompts were collected for service improvement (though not for training). For self-hosted Enterprise deployments with BYOK/BYOLLM, Sourcegraph does not receive prompts or responses at all - they stay entirely inside the customer perimeter. The default cloud Enterprise tier still routes through Sourcegraph's gateway to third-party LLM APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google), but with contractual ZDR and no-training guarantees.

suitability_notes.personal: Cloud Enterprise tier has contractual no-train + ZDR guarantees and SOC 2 Type II / GDPR compliance, which satisfies most GDPR-pseudonymised data requirements under a DPA - but only on the Enterprise plan. Self-hosted deployment with BYOK keeps data fully inside the organisation perimeter and is the stronger option for GDPR-sensitive research data.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-first: the AI runs in the vendor cloud by default (zdr). Only an enterprise on-prem / self-hosted deployment on infrastructure you control could suit special-category data — and only with DPO/IRB approval; the default cloud product is not suitable.

pricing: Enterprise only (as of July 23 2025): ~$59/user/month (annual contract); platform starting at $16K/year includes AI-feature credits. Free, Pro, and Enterprise Starter plans discontinued July 23 2025. No public academic or research discount tier - contact sales.
academic: No published academic or student discount. Free and Pro tiers were discontinued in July 2025. Researchers must purchase Enterprise ($59/user/month, annual). Contact sales for possible institutional pricing.
use_cases: Multi-repo codebase understanding and architecture queries
AI chat, inline edit, and autocomplete in VS Code, JetBrains, Visual Studio (experimental)
Context-aware code generation drawing on millions of lines across repositories
Debug, explain, and refactor code with enterprise codebase context
Large-scale research or bioinformatics pipelines with complex cross-repo dependencies
notes: Cody uniquely leverages Sourcegraph's multi-repo code search index to surface context at scale - a differentiator from file-scoped tools like Copilot or Cursor. As of mid-2026 the product is enterprise-only with no free tier, making it costly for small research groups. The self-hosted path (Docker/Kubernetes, BYOK, context filters) is the recommended route for ELIXIR or other research environments handling sensitive data. HIPAA-BAA availability is not publicly confirmed - requires direct sales engagement. 'runs_locally' is false because even self-hosted deployments route LLM inference to external provider APIs by default (unless a self-hosted/private model endpoint is configured via BYOLLM).

name: OpenCode
model_backend: BYO-key (75+ providers via Models.dev: Claude, GPT, Gemini, AWS Bedrock, Groq, Azure, OpenRouter, Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp and others); optional hosted gateway (Zen pay-as-you-go, Go $10/mo, Black enterprise)
data_handling_note: Core tool is a local MIT-licensed CLI/TUI - no code or context data is sent to OpenCode servers when using BYO-key mode; data goes only to the chosen LLM provider and is subject to that provider's policy. The optional Zen hosted gateway states providers follow a zero-retention policy and do not train on data, EXCEPT free/trial models (Big Pickle, DeepSeek V4 Flash Free, etc.) where data may be used to improve the model. The /share feature optionally sends conversation data to OpenCode's CDN - disableable in enterprise config. Free account ToS allows OpenCode to use content to improve services. A past privacy issue (session titles sent to cloud even when using local models) was flagged and reportedly resolved. No SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, or ISO 27001 certifications found. Default/free Zen models are risky for sensitive data.

suitability_notes.personal: Safe in fully local mode (BYO-key + local model via Ollama/llama.cpp/LM Studio) - data never leaves the machine. With hosted Zen/Go tiers using paid models (zero-retention policy), acceptable if the provider's no-training guarantee is verified per provider. Free Zen models explicitly allow data use for improvement - avoid for personal data. Enterprise Internal AI Gateway mode routes all requests through org infrastructure and disables external providers, making it suitable with appropriate org-level controls.
suitability_notes.special: Safe only if deployed fully locally: run opencode with a local model endpoint (Ollama, llama.cpp) and disable the /share feature and session-title cloud call. Enterprise Internal Gateway mode with self-hosted LLM also qualifies. No BAA, HIPAA, or clinical compliance certifications exist. Avoid all cloud/Zen tiers for special-category data.

pricing: Free (BYOK, full features, MIT open source); Zen pay-as-you-go ($20 pre-paid balance, zero markup, curated models); Go $5 first month then $10/month (curated open models, $60/month usage cap); Black enterprise per-seat custom pricing ($20/$100/$200 monthly tiers mentioned). No academic or student discount found. Claude Pro / ChatGPT Plus accounts can be connected at no extra OpenCode fee.
academic: No academic or student discount found as of June 2026. The free BYOK tier is available to all users.
use_cases: Local/offline AI coding with Ollama or llama.cpp
Multi-session parallel agents on same codebase
Enterprise coding agent with internal LLM gateway
MCP tool integration for coding agents
GitHub Copilot / ChatGPT Plus account integration
notes: The GitHub repository was previously at sst/opencode (SST team) and now lives at opencode-ai/opencode (anomalyco). An earlier archived project at the same name (a Go CLI tool archived September 2025) is a different/predecessor project - the active opencode.ai product launched in early 2026 and reached 172K GitHub stars by June 2026. The /share feature sends conversation data to OpenCode CDN and should be disabled for any sensitive use. Confidence is medium because: no direct enterprise DPA page was found, compliance certifications are absent from all official docs, and the exact data handling of the Zen Black enterprise tier could not be verified from public pages.

name: Crush
model_backend: BYOK: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Groq, AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Vertex AI, OpenRouter, Hugging Face, Cerebras, Fireworks, and any OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible endpoint; also local via Ollama, LM Studio, LiteLLM, MLX
data_handling_note: Crush is a local CLI (BYOK) - the tool itself only collects pseudonymous usage metrics tied to a device hash; prompts and responses are explicitly never collected by Charmbracelet. Opt-out via CRUSH_DISABLE_METRICS=1 or DO_NOT_TRACK=1. Supports fully air-gapped operation with local models (Ollama, LM Studio, LiteLLM, MLX). When using cloud LLM providers, data handling is governed by those providers. The optional managed Charm Hyper backend (free: 100 credits/month; $20/month: 250 daily credits) has its own privacy policy: prompts/outputs never used to train models, content not stored by default (max 30 days if retained for debugging), DPA available on request for EEA/UK/Swiss users, GDPR-compliant via Standard Contractual Clauses. No SOC 2, ISO 27001, or HIPAA BAA found for Hyper. Default free tier of Hyper routes content through cloud; local-only deployment is the safest path for sensitive data.

suitability_notes.personal: Use local models (Ollama, LM Studio) or air-gapped mode to keep GDPR-regulated data fully on-device - no data leaves the machine. If using cloud providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) or Charm Hyper, review each provider's DPA; Hyper offers a DPA on request for EEA/UK/Swiss users but has no SOC 2 or HIPAA BAA. Local deployment with CRUSH_DISABLE_METRICS=1 is the recommended configuration.
suitability_notes.special: Run exclusively with local models (Ollama + Qwen Coder, DeepSeek Coder, etc.) in fully air-gapped mode. Crush supports air-gapped operation with offline provider snapshots (crush update-providers embedded). No data leaves the machine in this configuration. Cloud-provider or Charm Hyper backends must not be used for health/genetic/clinical data as no BAA or clinical compliance certification is available.

compliance: GDPR (Charm Hyper backend, via SCCs; DPA on request for EEA/UK/CH)
pricing: Crush CLI is free and open source (FSL-1.1-MIT). No charge from Charmbracelet; you pay your chosen LLM provider's API rates. Optional Charm Hyper managed backend: free tier (100 Hypercredits/month), $20/month subscription (250 daily refreshing credits), prepaid bundles ($5=100 credits, $10=500 credits, $20=2000 credits; 1 Hypercredit = $0.05). Local model use (Ollama etc.) has zero ongoing cost.
academic: No academic or student pricing exists for Crush itself. The CLI is free to all users. Academic discounts on LLM API costs depend on individual provider programs (e.g., Google, Anthropic, OpenAI). Local model use is free and suitable for academic environments.
use_cases: Agentic code generation and editing in the terminal
LSP-enhanced code context for AI prompting
Local/offline coding with privacy-sensitive code via Ollama
Multi-model mid-session switching for cost/quality tradeoffs
Shared backend workspaces via crush serve (experimental)
notes: FSL-1.1-MIT is a time-limited source-available license (not OSI-approved open source) that converts to MIT after 2 years; commercial use has restrictions during the FSL period. The crush serve shared backend is marked experimental. Model capability is entirely backend-dependent - with frontier models (Claude 3.7 Sonnet, GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro) it is frontier-class; with smaller local models it may be basic. Classified as frontier to reflect the ceiling with BYOK. The rebranding controversy (summer 2025) resulted in two divergent projects: Crush (Charmbracelet) and the community-maintained OpenCode; both are active as of June 2026.

name: Mistral Vibe
model_backend: Devstral 2 (123B, open-weight, modified MIT) and Devstral Small 2 (24B, Apache 2.0) by default; Mistral Medium 3.5 for remote agents; any OpenAI-compatible local endpoint configurable (Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp, LM Studio)
data_handling_note: Local/self-host is the best available option: Vibe CLI can be fully pointed at a local Ollama/vLLM endpoint running Devstral Small 2 (24B, Apache 2.0), with telemetry disabled, so no data ever leaves the machine. When using Mistral's cloud API: free-tier users may have prompts used for model training (opt-out available in account settings); paid API and Le Chat Pro/Team/Enterprise users are excluded from training by default; Zero Data Retention (ZDR) is available on the Scale/API tier for stateless calls only - it does NOT apply to Vibe's agentic/stateful sessions; cloud API logs are kept 30 rolling days for abuse monitoring unless ZDR is active. EU data residency by default (Mistral is Paris-headquartered, data stored in EU). The free (cloud) default is risky for sensitive data - training opt-out is not automatic on the free tier.

suitability_notes.personal: Run fully locally with Devstral Small 2 via Ollama/vLLM (telemetry disabled) for automatic ok; OR use paid API tier (Pro/Team/Enterprise) which excludes data from training and has a DPA available - still cloud-transit risk. Free cloud tier is risky by default.
suitability_notes.special: Run fully locally with Devstral Small 2 (24B, Apache 2.0) via Ollama/vLLM with telemetry disabled - data never leaves the machine. Self-hosted Devstral (open-weight) on institutional infrastructure also qualifies. Cloud tiers (including Enterprise) are not suitable for special-category data without explicit contractual guarantees; no HIPAA BAA or clinical-use commitment is documented.

pricing: Free: basic access, ~25 msgs/day, may train on data; Pro: $14.99/month (includes Vibe coding, remote agents, no-telemetry mode, excluded from training); Team: $24.99/user/month (shared workspace, 30GB storage, admin controls); Enterprise: custom (private deployment, SAML SSO, audit logs, model fine-tuning); API: pay-per-token (Devstral 2: $0.40/$2.00 per M tokens in/out; Devstral Small 2: $0.10/$0.30 per M tokens); Student: 5.99/month (60% off Pro, verified students at accredited HEI, up to 12 months, new accounts only, no API credits included)
academic: Student plan at 5.99/month (vs. 14.99 standard Pro), valid up to 12 months for verified students at accredited higher education institutions. Requires institutional email and ID verification. New accounts only. Does not include API token credits - IDE/script use billed separately. Additionally, Devstral Small 2 (Apache 2.0) can be downloaded and self-hosted for free with no subscription required.
use_cases: IDE-integrated coding assistance (VS Code, JetBrains, Zed)
Local/air-gapped coding with self-hosted Devstral Small 2
notes: Mistral Vibe is open-core: the CLI itself is Apache 2.0 (github.com/mistralai/mistral-vibe, v2.17.1 as of June 2026, 4.5k stars); Devstral Small 2 (24B) is Apache 2.0 and the recommended local model; Devstral 2 (123B) uses a modified MIT license. The tool was previously known as part of the Le Chat ecosystem and rebranded/expanded as Mistral Vibe in 2026, unifying work and coding agents. EU-headquartered vendor (Paris) with data stored in EU by default - a meaningful differentiator for GDPR-regulated research environments. Compliance: SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001/27701 confirmed; HIPAA compliance is claimed by some third-party sources but not confirmed in Mistral's own help center article on certifications. FedRAMP is similarly unconfirmed from primary sources.

name: Kiro CLI
model_backend: Amazon Bedrock (Anthropic Claude Opus 4.8/4.7/4.6/4.5, Sonnet 4.6/4.5/4.0, Haiku 4.5; open-weight models: MiniMax M2.5/M2.1, GLM-5, DeepSeek 3.2, Qwen3 Coder Next; Auto router default)
data_handling_note: Free tier and individual social-login users: content (prompts, responses, code) may be used for service improvement with opt-out available; data stored up to 60 days in US East (N. Virginia). Pro/Pro+/Pro Max/Power users authenticated via AWS IAM Identity Center or external IdP (Okta, Entra ID): content NOT used for service improvement. AWS GovCloud regions: service-improvement content collection is disabled by default for all users. Enterprise (team) tier: data reportedly not stored. The safe no-train option therefore requires a paid individual tier with IAM Identity Center auth, or enterprise/GovCloud deployment - the free-tier default is risky (content used for improvement).

suitability_notes.personal: Requires paid tier (Pro or above) with authentication via AWS IAM Identity Center or an external IdP (Okta, Microsoft Entra ID) to disable content use for service improvement. Free-tier and social-login users are not protected by default. GDPR compliance is not explicitly certified, but AWS-standard data protections apply and a no-train path exists at enterprise tier.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

compliance: HIPAA (explicitly listed for Kiro IDE and CLI)
AWS shared-responsibility model (SOC 2/ISO 27001 via AWS infrastructure - confirm scope via AWS Artifact)
AWS KMS at rest (customer-managed keys available for enterprise)
pricing: Free: 50 credits/month (Claude Sonnet 4.5-class, open-weight models, no training only if IAM IdP auth); Pro: $20/month (1,000 credits); Pro+: $40/month (2,000 credits); Pro Max: $100/month (5,000 credits); Power: $200/month (10,000 credits). Overage: $0.04/credit across all paid tiers. GovCloud pricing ~20% higher; free tier unavailable there. Startup program: up to 1 year of free Pro+. Enterprise: custom/contact sales with centralized billing and organizational dashboard.
academic: Students from 11 eligible universities (including Arizona State, Carnegie Mellon, Georgia Tech, NYU, University of Waterloo) receive 1,000 credits/month free for one year via the Kiro for Students program; verified through SheerID with a university email (.edu/.ca); no credit card required; converts to free tier after one year. See kiro.dev/students/.
use_cases: Terminal-based AI-assisted coding and chat (spec-driven development)
Custom agentic workflows via hooks and custom agents
MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration with external tools and data sources
CI/CD pipeline automation with headless API-key auth
Migration path from Amazon Q Developer CLI (backward-compatible q and q chat entry points)
notes: Kiro CLI installs via curl on macOS/Linux/Windows and is backward-compatible with the q and q chat entry points from Amazon Q Developer CLI. It uses spec-driven agentic development (requirements.md, design.md, tasks.md generated before coding), supports parallel agents, property-based testing, custom hooks, MCP integration, and CI/CD headless automation. The Auto router selects the optimal model per task; free tier gets Sonnet-class routing, paid tiers get Opus-class. The tool is cloud-only - inference runs on Amazon Bedrock, no local model execution. AWS Artifact can be used to access third-party audit reports. Data residency: free/individual in US East (N. Virginia); GovCloud stays within GovCloud regions; EU-region support via AWS Frankfurt for enterprise IAM Identity Center.

name: Warp
deployment: cloud / self-hostable (execution layer only; orchestration remains Warp-cloud)
model_backend: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Amazon Bedrock, LiteLLM, OpenRouter, custom inference; BYOK supported; Enterprise allows BYOLLM
data_handling_note: Zero Data Retention (ZDR) with Warp's LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) is available on the Enterprise plan only. On the Business plan, team-wide ZDR enforcement is mentioned for Warp AI. Free tier users can opt out of telemetry in Settings but AI features may require data to be sent to Warp servers; the privacy page notes telemetry is required for AI features on the free tier. The default free/Build plan routes AI prompts through Warp's proxy to US-hosted LLM APIs without guaranteed ZDR - this is the risky default. Paid Business plan ($50/user/month) unlocks team-wide ZDR enforcement; Enterprise adds self-hosted execution and BYOLLM. The privacy policy explicitly states Warp may train/fine-tune models on user data unless ZDR is in effect.

suitability_notes.personal: Personal/pseudonymised data (GDPR-sensitive) requires Business plan ($50/user/month) or Enterprise for team-wide Zero Data Retention enforcement and a signed DPA (GDPR SCCs available via Data Processing Addendum). Default free/Build tier routes prompts through Warp servers to US-based LLMs without ZDR - do not use with personal data at those tiers.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only for AI processing. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

pricing: Free ($0, limited AI credits, limited cloud agents); Build ($20/month or $18/month annual, 1,500 AI credits, BYOK, full model access); Max ($200/month or $180/month annual, 18,000 credits); Business ($50/user/month or $45/user/month annual, up to 50 seats, team ZDR, SAML SSO); Enterprise (custom pricing, self-hosted execution, BYOLLM, white-glove onboarding). No documented academic or student discount; a Campus Lead ambassador program exists but provides no direct pricing benefit.
academic: No formal academic or student discount. Free tier includes full terminal features and limited AI. A Campus Lead program exists for student evangelists but does not reduce pricing. No evidence of educational institution deals.
use_cases: Terminal-native agentic coding and code generation
Multi-agent orchestration via Oz platform (webhook/CI-triggered cloud agents)
Codebase indexing and search across large codebases
Running and orchestrating external CLI agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI)
Automated PR creation, issue triage, spec writing via cloud agents
Team-wide AI-assisted terminal workflows with centralized governance
BYOK / BYOLLM enterprise deployments with self-hosted execution
notes: Warp went open source (client) in April/May 2026 under AGPL v3 (backend/orchestration remains proprietary cloud). The terminal itself is free and runs locally on macOS and Linux. AI/agent features require cloud connectivity to Warp's proxy for LLM routing - there is no fully offline/air-gapped mode even for Enterprise (orchestration still touches Warp cloud). Self-hosted execution (Enterprise) keeps code and workspaces on customer infrastructure but orchestration goes through Warp. The privacy policy explicitly reserves the right to fine-tune models on user data absent ZDR. Data is stored on Google Cloud Platform (US). No EU data residency option documented. The product reportedly processed 3.2 billion lines of code in 2025 via cloud agents.

name: Augment Code
model_backend: Proprietary multi-model routing (Augment Context Engine + Claude Opus/Sonnet, GPT-5.5, Gemini Flash, Kimi K2 via configurable "Prism" router); BYO-key (BYOK) supported in Enterprise
data_handling_note: Zero data retention (ZDR) with all contracted LLM providers is enforced on Business and Enterprise tiers; Enterprise also adds CMEK encryption, SSO/SCIM, ISO 42001, and HIPAA BAA. The free Trial tier and Indie plan ($20/mo) do NOT carry explicit ZDR guarantees and should be considered risky for any sensitive data. The vendor explicitly states customer code is never used for AI training across all tiers, but ZDR (log-level retention) requires Business or Enterprise. On-premises / VPC / single-tenant and self-hosted deployment are available as Enterprise options, removing cloud exposure entirely.

suitability_notes.personal: GDPR-compliant with DPA and Standard Contractual Clauses available; ZDR requires Business or Enterprise tier. On-premises/VPC deployment (Enterprise) keeps data fully in-house. Default Trial/Indie tier lacks explicit retention controls and should not be used for pseudonymised personal data without verifying DPA terms.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-first: the AI runs in the vendor cloud by default (zdr). Only an enterprise on-prem / self-hosted deployment on infrastructure you control could suit special-category data — and only with DPO/IRB approval; the default cloud product is not suitable.

pricing: Free trial (30,000 credits, credit card required, one-time); Indie $20/mo (40,000 credits, 1 user); Standard $60/dev/mo (130,000 credits, up to 20 users, Slack, PR reviews); Max $200/dev/mo (450,000 credits, up to 20 users); Enterprise: custom pricing, unlimited users, SSO/SCIM/OIDC, CMEK, ISO 42001, VPC/on-prem, dedicated SLA. Auto top-up: $15 per 24,000 credits (valid 12 months). No permanent free tier; no academic discount publicly listed (open-source maintainers may qualify for free access).
academic: No dedicated academic or student discount is publicly listed. Open-source contributors/maintainers may qualify for complimentary access. Researchers should contact Augment sales to inquire about research or institutional pricing.
use_cases: Agentic codebase-aware code generation and editing via terminal
Large codebase indexing (400k+ files) with semantic Context Engine
CI/CD automation and headless PR review in GitHub Actions / Jenkins
Multi-agent coordination (triage, implementation, PR authoring, code review)
MCP-based integration with GitHub, Linear, Jira, and external tools
Parallel sub-agent workflows for security audits and test generation
Enterprise software development at scale with compliance requirements
notes: Auggie CLI (npm: @augmentcode/auggie) is the terminal/CLI face of the Augment Code platform, which also includes the Cosmos multi-agent cloud platform and IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains, Zed). It requires Node.js 20+ and an Augment account (OAuth login). The CLI itself runs locally but routes all AI inference to Augment's cloud. The Context Engine indexes codebases semantically (not grep-based) and can handle 400k+ file repos. Multi-model routing via "Prism" selects among Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Kimi depending on task. BYOK is an Enterprise feature enabling use of the organisation's own API keys with their own DPAs. The GitHub repo (github.com/augmentcode/auggie) appears to be source-available or proprietary - not confirmed open-source. Non-interactive/headless CI mode is an Enterprise-tier feature. Trust center URL is https://trust.augmentcode.com (returned 404 at time of check; security page at /security was accessible and confirmed HIPAA/GDPR/SOC2/ISO42001).

name: Plandex
vendor: plandex-ai (MIT open-source; Plandex Cloud wound down November 2025)
model_backend: BYO-key: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama (local), OpenRouter, any OpenAI-compatible provider
data_handling_note: In self-hosted/local mode (the only mode now that Plandex Cloud is shut down), no data is sent to Plandex infrastructure. Data goes only to the user-chosen model provider (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama locally). With Ollama and local open-source models, nothing leaves the machine at all. Former Plandex Cloud DID retain user data and stated it might be used for model training - but the cloud is gone. The default self-hosted path is private by design; the DEFAULT IS SAFE.

suitability_notes.personal: Self-hosted with a cloud model provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) requires verifying that provider's DPA/no-train terms before using GDPR personal data. With local Ollama models, data never leaves the machine - fully ok for personal data.
suitability_notes.special: Fully local mode (self-hosted Plandex server + Ollama with a capable local model) keeps all data on-premises and is suitable for special-category data. Using cloud model providers with special-category data requires a DPA with that provider. No vendor-level HIPAA-BAA or compliance certifications exist for Plandex itself.

pricing: Free and open-source (MIT). No Plandex subscription fee. Users pay only their own model provider API costs (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI). Typical heavy usage: $20-$100/month in API costs depending on model choice. Ollama with local models is cost-free beyond hardware.
academic: No dedicated academic tier or discount. The tool itself is free (MIT); academic researchers benefit from BYO institutional API keys and from the zero-cost fully-local Ollama path.
use_cases: Long multi-file coding tasks spanning dozens of files
Autonomous code generation and refactoring for large research codebases
Terminal-based agentic coding with diff-review sandbox for change approval
Self-hosted/air-gapped coding agent using local models via Ollama
Multi-step autonomous debugging of build/test/linter pipelines
Reproducible plan-based development with branching and version control
notes: Plandex v2.2.1 (July 2025) is the latest release. Key differentiator for researchers: up to 2M token effective context window, tree-sitter project maps supporting codebases with 20M+ tokens, a diff-review sandbox that keeps all AI changes pending until explicitly approved, configurable autonomy levels (step-by-step to fully autonomous), plan branching and version control, and native Ollama support for fully local operation. No IDE plugin yet (VS Code integration in development). No MCP support as of latest release. MIT license - code can be freely inspected, modified, and deployed in institutional infrastructure.

name: Jules
model_backend: Gemini 2.5 Pro (fixed; some sources note Gemini 3 Pro in newer releases)
data_handling_note: Google states private code is not used to train models; tasks run in isolated Google Cloud VMs destroyed after completion. Public repositories may be used for training. This no-train commitment applies to all tiers including free, but it is a policy statement rather than a contractual DPA. Data retention periods for cloned repos and execution logs are not publicly documented. No Jules-specific SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA, or GDPR DPA are published; formal compliance documentation must be requested via enterprise channels. The free/consumer tier is therefore still risky for sensitive data despite the no-train claim.

suitability_notes.personal: Google states private code is not used for training and VMs are destroyed after tasks, but no Jules-specific DPA, GDPR data processing agreement, or documented data retention SLA is publicly available. Processing occurs in Google Cloud (US by default, no published EU residency option for Jules).
