Suitable only if your institution can accept Google's general privacy terms and you treat it as a no-train cloud processor; enterprise customers should request a formal DPA.
suitability_notes.special: No self-hosting or on-premises deployment option exists; all code is cloned to Google Cloud VMs. No HIPAA BAA specific to Jules is published. Health/genetic/clinical data must not be processed in Jules in any tier.

compliance: Google Cloud holds SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA BAA but Jules is a Google Labs product not explicitly covered
GDPR DPA must be requested via enterprise channels
pricing: Free: 15 tasks/day, 3 concurrent (no cost). Google AI Pro: $19.99/month, 100 tasks/day, 15 concurrent. Google AI Ultra: $124.99/month, 300 tasks/day, 60 concurrent. Jules is bundled into Google AI Pro and Ultra subscription plans.
academic: College students in eligible countries (US, Japan, Indonesia, South Korea, Brazil and expanding) can get Google AI Pro free for 12 months via a student verification program, which includes Jules with higher task limits. After 12 months, standard pricing applies.
use_cases: Multi-file code changes via pull requests
Async coding tasks in background while developer works on other things
notes: Jules is a pure-cloud asynchronous coding agent: it clones a GitHub repo into a Google Cloud VM, executes multi-file changes, and opens a PR. It is not an IDE plugin and does not run locally. The CLI (npm install -g @google/jules) and REST API (alpha) allow automation and CI/CD integration. The GitHub Action (google-labs-code/jules-action) enables triggering Jules from workflows including on CI failures. Capability is rated frontier due to Gemini 2.5 Pro backend and ~51.8% SWE-bench Verified score. The main privacy concern for ELIXIR researchers is the lack of published Jules-specific DPA, GDPR data processing agreement, data retention SLA, and EU data residency controls. Do not process personal or special-category data without a formal contractual arrangement with Google.

name: SWE-agent
model_backend: Any LLM via litellm (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama local models, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, OpenRouter, Portkey); BYO API key
data_handling_note: SWE-agent itself collects no data - it is a local CLI tool with no cloud backend. Data handling depends entirely on the LLM provider chosen. With local models (e.g., Ollama), data never leaves the machine. With cloud providers (OpenAI, Anthropic), data is subject to that provider's privacy terms. The default free tier is safe only if using a local/self-hosted LLM; using cloud APIs routes code through third-party servers. Princeton/Stanford operate no SaaS and have no privacy policy for the tool itself. Note: as of 2025-2026, the project is in maintenance-only mode; active development has shifted to mini-SWE-agent.

suitability_notes.personal: Safe when configured to use a local model (Ollama + open-weight LLM) so no data transits external APIs. If using cloud LLM backends (OpenAI, Anthropic), personal/pseudonymised data would be processed under those providers' terms - requires verifying no-training opt-out or enterprise DPA with the chosen provider.
suitability_notes.special: Safe only when running with a fully local/self-hosted LLM (e.g., Ollama on-prem) - the tool itself imposes no external data transfer. Cloud LLM backends are not acceptable for special-category data without explicit BAA/DPA and no-training guarantees from that provider. Self-host path exists out of the box with Ollama/litellm.

pricing: Free and open-source (MIT). No SaaS tier. Users pay only for the LLM API they configure (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI tokens). Local model use incurs no API cost.
academic: Developed by academic researchers at Princeton University (and collaborators from Stanford, Tel Aviv University, NYU). Published at NeurIPS 2024 (Yang et al., "SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering", arXiv:2405.15793). Designed explicitly for research use; batch benchmarking mode supports SWE-bench evaluation. No dedicated academic discount needed - fully free.
use_cases: Parallelized cloud code execution via SWE-ReX
Customizable agent harness via YAML for research experimentation
notes: SWE-agent is now superseded by mini-SWE-agent (https://mini-swe-agent.com) for most use cases - the original SWE-agent remains better for experimenting with diverse tool sets and custom history processors. The tool achieved 12.29% on SWE-bench full at launch; with Claude 3.7, v1.0 achieved SOTA on SWE-bench full and verified. EnIGMA mode (cybersecurity) developed with Tel Aviv University and NYU. SWE-ReX enables parallelized cloud execution on Modal, AWS Fargate, and similar services. Wiki entry should note maintenance-only status and point to mini-SWE-agent as the active successor.

name: gptme
model_backend: Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI (GPT), Google (Gemini), xAI (Grok), DeepSeek, OpenRouter (100+ models), Azure, local via Ollama / llama-cpp-python
data_handling_note: gptme itself stores no data remotely - all conversations are written to local directories (~/.local/share/gptme) or self-hosted Docker volumes. Data sent to LLM providers depends entirely on which provider the user configures and that provider's own policies. With local models (Ollama, llama-cpp-python) nothing leaves the machine. With cloud providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) the user's prompts travel to those vendors under their own privacy terms. The tool itself has no telemetry, no SaaS tier, no vendor data collection. OpenRouter defaults to data_collection=deny, blocking provider training. No compliance certifications exist for the tool itself (MIT open-source project, not a SaaS vendor).

suitability_notes.personal: Safe when run with a local model (Ollama/llama-cpp-python) or an enterprise API provider configured with a DPA and no-train terms (e.g. Anthropic API default does not train; OpenAI API no-train by default). Cloud provider keys must be reviewed individually. The tool itself does not retain or share data.
suitability_notes.special: Can handle special-category data only when running fully locally with a local model backend - data never leaves the machine in that configuration. Cloud provider backends are not suitable without a sector-specific agreement (BAA for health data etc.) from the chosen provider, which gptme itself cannot guarantee.

pricing: Free and open-source (MIT). No subscription tiers. API costs are paid directly to whichever LLM provider the user chooses. Running with local models (Ollama, llama-cpp-python) incurs no API costs at all.
academic: No academic discount program exists - the tool itself is free. Researchers at institutions with cloud LLM credits (e.g. Anthropic research access, Azure academic agreements) can apply those directly.
use_cases: Autonomous coding agent in the terminal
Literature review and web research automation (built-in researcher subagent profile)
File editing and patching via shell or Python
Self-hosted LLM orchestration on laptops, HPC login nodes, or CI/CD
MCP tool integration and multi-agent orchestration
notes: gptme is the most privacy-friendly category of tool for ELIXIR researchers: the software itself is open-source with no telemetry or vendor lock-in. The critical privacy variable is which LLM provider API key the researcher uses. For sensitive data, the recommended configuration is local model inference (Ollama or llama-cpp-python via the local/ provider prefix), in which case no data leaves the machine at all. The built-in 'researcher' subagent profile (tools: browser, read) supports automated literature review without modifying files. The gptme-agent-template v0.4 supports persistent autonomous agents with safety guardrails (GREEN/YELLOW/RED operation classification).

name: Open Interpreter
model_backend: BYO: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI, AWS SageMaker; local: Ollama, LM Studio, LlamaFile, Jan.ai; + 15+ hosted via LiteLLM (Groq, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Qwen, etc.)
data_handling_note: When running with local models (Ollama, LM Studio, LlamaFile) and `interpreter.offline = True`, no data leaves the machine - this is the safest path. Default anonymous usage telemetry (version, run mode, exceptions - NOT prompts or code) is sent to PostHog; disable with `--disable_telemetry` flag or env var. For hosted Pro/Business tiers, prompts and attachments are forwarded to the chosen AI provider under their own privacy terms; Open Interpreter retains hosted request logs up to 30 days for safety/debugging then deletes them. The privacy policy (last updated Oct 25, 2024) states conversations are anonymized before being used to improve their AI model, but no explicit opt-out for this hosted-tier training use was found. No DPA, SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA-BAA, or GDPR certification documented. Default free tier (BYO key, local model) is safe; hosted tiers carry provider-dependent risks.

suitability_notes.personal: Safe only in local mode: set `interpreter.offline = True` with an Ollama/LM Studio backend - no data leaves the machine. Hosted Pro/Business tiers forward prompts to AI providers and retain logs 30 days; no DPA or GDPR-compliant data processing agreement found for hosted tiers. Do not use hosted tier for GDPR-scoped personal data.
suitability_notes.special: Safe only in fully local/air-gapped configuration: Ollama + `interpreter.offline = True` keeps health/genetic data on-premise. No vendor BAA, HIPAA compliance, or clinical-use certification exists. Docker sandboxing is available (experimental) for additional isolation. E2B sandboxing is cloud-based and not suitable. For special-category data, the self-hosted local-model path is required and must be explicitly configured.

pricing: Free: open-source CLI, BYO API keys or Ollama local models (pip install). Pro: $20/month (hosted models, 4x usage, document editing, priority support). Business: $60/month (team management, 3x usage over Pro). No academic or student discount found.
academic: No academic or student discount programme found as of June 2026. The open-source CLI is freely available to all users with BYO API keys or local models at no cost.
use_cases: Local code execution and automation via natural language
Reading, editing, and creating files (Python, Word, Excel, PDF)
Data analysis and plotting on local datasets
Batch file operations and folder organisation
Running shell commands and scripts under LLM guidance
Embedding an LLM agent in custom Python scripts (headless/API mode)
Computer/OS control (mouse, keyboard, screen) via interpreter --os
notes: The vendor website (openinterpreter.com) renders as a single-page React app that WebFetch cannot parse beyond the title, so privacy policy content was reconstructed from Google snippet text and cached search results. The claim that conversations are anonymized before AI model improvement comes from search snippets referencing the Oct 2024 policy; the full policy text was not directly readable. Telemetry details come from official docs at docs.openinterpreter.com which were accessible. There are two active GitHub repos: openinterpreter/open-interpreter (main, Apache-2.0, ~60k stars) and the newer openinterpreter/openinterpreter (lightweight agent for open models like DeepSeek/Kimi/Qwen). The lead description's claim of 'Apache 2.0' is confirmed; some third-party reviews incorrectly list AGPL. No evidence of acquisition, rebrand, or discontinuation.

name: Kilo Code
model_backend: BYOK (500+ models via OpenRouter / direct providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.) or local via Ollama / LM Studio / Atomic Chat; optional Kilo Gateway (pay-as-you-go, no markup)
data_handling_note: Best option is fully local: with Ollama/LM Studio code never leaves the machine and Kilo itself processes nothing. For cloud-API paths (BYOK or Kilo Gateway): Kilo's own privacy doc says it does not store or process prompts/code, but the underlying AI provider may retain data per their own policies. Zero Data Retention (ZDR) routing via OpenRouter is available but currently requires a Teams plan ($15/user/month) or Enterprise - it was removed from individual/Kilo Pass tiers (regression confirmed in GitHub discussion #6015). The Terms of Service grant Kilo a broad licence to use Customer Data "to provide and improve the Service"; model-training opt-out is available but may restrict model choices. The free/individual tier is therefore moderately risky for sensitive data in cloud-API mode; local-model mode is safe at any tier. SOC 2 Type I is claimed (landing page) but no audit report or trust portal URL was found. No HIPAA BAA, no ISO 27001; ToS explicitly prohibits sending HIPAA/PCI/GLBA-regulated data via the cloud service.

suitability_notes.personal: Safe if configured to use fully local models (Ollama/LM Studio - code never leaves the machine). In cloud-API mode, ZDR routing requires Teams ($15/user/month) or Enterprise plan; the free and Kilo Pass tiers do not provide ZDR. A DPA can apparently be obtained on request (privacy policy references SCCs) but is not self-service.
suitability_notes.special: cfg path requires running entirely with local models (Ollama + LanceDB local vector store) so data never reaches any external server, including Kilo's own. Cloud paths are prohibited by ToS for HIPAA/clinical data. No HIPAA BAA is available. Full local setup is technically feasible but requires capable hardware and manual configuration.

compliance: SOC 2 Type I (claimed on landing page, no public report found)
pricing: Free (open-source extension + CLI, BYOK); $20 free credits on signup; Kilo Gateway pay-as-you-go at exact provider rates (zero markup); Kilo Pass subscription $19/month (Starter), $49/month (Pro), $199/month (Expert); Teams $15/user/month (14-day trial); Enterprise custom pricing. KiloClaw (managed cloud agent hosting) $55/month separately.
academic: No academic or student discount programme found on the pricing page. The free tier (BYOK + $20 credits) and open-source nature make it accessible without a special discount.
use_cases: Agentic code generation and multi-file editing in VS Code and JetBrains
Automated code review with inline line-level comments
Multi-agent parallel task execution via Agent Manager and git worktrees
Local/air-gap coding with Ollama or LM Studio for privacy-sensitive work
CLI-based agentic coding and automation pipelines
Cloud agents and Slack-integrated agents for 24/7 autonomous tasks
Model comparison and cost benchmarking across 500+ providers
notes: SOC 2 Type I claim appears only on the marketing landing page; no public trust portal (e.g. trust.kilo.ai) was accessible during research. The terms-of-service broad licence clause and removal of ZDR from individual tiers are the two primary risk signals for researchers handling personal or special-category data. The fully local path (Ollama + LanceDB) is well-documented and is the recommended configuration for ELIXIR-sensitive workloads. The CLI is built on OpenCode (MIT). A 'legacy' repo (kilocode-legacy) exists on GitHub, suggesting an architecture migration occurred.

name: Google Colab (Data Science Agent)
model_backend: Gemini 2.0 (Google-hosted; not user-selectable)
data_handling_note: Critical tier split. FREE Colab: AI feature prompts, outputs, and related data ARE used to improve Google products and machine learning models; human reviewers may read submissions; data retained anonymised for up to 18 months with no deletion possible. PAID Colab Pro/Pro+: same consumer privacy terms apply - no explicit contractual no-train guarantee. COLAB ENTERPRISE (Google Cloud): governed by Gemini for Google Cloud data governance - Google explicitly does NOT use prompts or responses to train models; data encrypted in transit and at rest; Data Processing Addendum available; VPC Service Controls, CMEK, and Access Transparency available. The 'no-train' rating reflects the Enterprise/Cloud tier; the free tier is risky for any research data.

suitability_notes.personal: Personal/pseudonymised data (GDPR) requires Colab Enterprise tier under a Google Cloud DPA. Free and paid consumer tiers (Pro/Pro+) use AI feature data for product improvement and permit human review - not suitable for GDPR-regulated personal data by default. Unlock: use Colab Enterprise with an active Google Cloud Data Processing Addendum and VPC Service Controls.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

compliance: HIPAA BAA (via Google Cloud, Enterprise tier)
GDPR DPA (Google Cloud Data Processing Addendum)
pricing: Free tier: hosted Jupyter notebooks, limited GPU/TPU, 12-hour runtime, Data Science Agent available (consumer privacy terms apply). Colab Pro: ~$10-12/month, priority GPU (T4/P100), 24-hour runtime, 100 GB Drive. Colab Pro+: ~$50/month, A100/V100 GPU, high priority, background execution. Colab Pro free for students and teachers (1-year subscription introduced late 2025). Colab Enterprise (Google Cloud): pay-as-you-go; Data Science Agent billed per input/output tokens at BigQuery Agents pricing; underlying compute billed separately via GCP. $300 free GCP trial credit available.
academic: Google introduced a free 1-year Colab Pro subscription for students and educators (announced 2025). University partnerships expanded at Google I/O 2025. Free tier remains available to all. Institutional Google Workspace education accounts may have different data handling under Workspace EDU terms.
use_cases: Automated EDA (exploratory data analysis) from natural language prompts
Full Python notebook generation for data cleaning, visualisation, predictive modelling
BigQuery-scale data processing with DataFrames and BigQuery ML
Interactive chart generation (histogram, scatter, box plots)
Reproducible research notebooks with Gemini-generated code
Model training and evaluation within notebook environment
notes: The product exists in two distinct deployment contexts that have very different privacy profiles: (1) consumer Google Colab (free/Pro/Pro+) accessed via colab.research.google.com - Gemini AI features are subject to consumer data-usage terms including potential training and human review; (2) Colab Enterprise accessed via Google Cloud Console - governed by Google Cloud enterprise terms with explicit no-train commitment, DPA, and compliance certifications. The Data Science Agent is available in both but under markedly different data governance regimes. For ELIXIR researchers: free Colab is unsuitable for any GDPR-regulated or special-category data; Colab Enterprise with a signed DPA and BAA is the minimum baseline for personal data and the only viable path for clinical/health data (still cloud-only, no air-gap). The tool does not run locally under any configuration.

name: Deepnote
deployment: cloud / self-hostable (single-tenancy enterprise option; local IDE extensions available)
model_backend: OpenAI (GPT-5), Anthropic (Sonnet 4.6); BYO-LLM on Enterprise tier
data_handling_note: Deepnote explicitly states processed content is not used for model-training purposes across all tiers. AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic) are used under enterprise API agreements that prohibit training on customer data. OpenAI may retain context up to 30 days for safety monitoring; Anthropic operates under zero-data-retention agreements for real-time suggestions. Block execution outputs are NOT sent to AI providers by default - users must explicitly enable that toggle. The free tier shares this no-train guarantee but has limited AI access. Enterprise tier adds BYO-LLM support (bring your own API key). DEFAULT free tier is lower risk than typical cloud tools because outputs are opt-in, but data still transits to US-based AI providers.

suitability_notes.personal: Personal/pseudonymised data (GDPR): DPA is available to all customer tiers (not enterprise-only), incorporates EU Standard Contractual Clauses (2021/914), and covers GDPR/UK GDPR/Swiss FADP. No-train policy is stated. However, data is stored and processed in the US (no EU data residency guarantee by default). cfg = use with a signed DPA, disable block-output sharing, and confirm your team plan includes DPA coverage. Enterprise single-tenancy with possible EU residency (contact sales) would upgrade this to closer to ok.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-first: the AI runs in the vendor cloud by default (no-train). Only an enterprise on-prem / self-hosted deployment on infrastructure you control could suit special-category data — and only with DPO/IRB approval; the default cloud product is not suitable.

pricing: Free tier: up to 3 editors, 5 projects, limited AI, unlimited basic compute (5 GB RAM/2 vCPU), 7-day revision history. Team: $39/editor/month (annual billing, 20% discount); unlimited editors/viewers/notebooks, GPT-5 + Sonnet 4.6 AI access, premium DB integrations (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift), scheduled runs, 30-day revision history, bundled compute credits. Enterprise: custom pricing; all Team features plus SSO, audit logs, HIPAA compliance, single-tenancy, BYO-LLM, dedicated success manager, volume discounts.
academic: No formal academic/student discount program listed on pricing page. GitHub README mentions free access for students and educators - likely available via application; not surfaced in main pricing UI. No dedicated academic tier confirmed.
use_cases: Collaborative data science notebooks (Python, R, SQL)
AI-assisted data analysis and code generation (Deepnote Agent / Auto AI)
Data team workflows with real-time multiplayer editing
Database querying and exploration (100+ native integrations)
Scheduled notebook execution and background jobs
Deploying data apps and API endpoints from notebooks
Version-controlled notebooks with YAML format (.deepnote) for git workflows
Local notebook development with VS Code/Cursor/Windsurf extensions
notes: The .deepnote open-source layer (Apache 2.0) covers the YAML notebook format, block type definitions (@deepnote/blocks), bidirectional conversion tooling (@deepnote/convert), and IDE extensions for VS Code, Cursor, and Windsurf. The cloud collaboration platform, Deepnote Agent, compute, and data integrations remain proprietary. runs_locally=true refers to local IDE extensions and the open-source format/tooling; full platform features require cloud. Block execution outputs are not sent to AI providers by default - this is a meaningful privacy control vs. most competing cloud notebook tools. Single-tenancy enterprise deployment exists but EU-region guarantee must be confirmed with sales. ISO 27001 and FedRAMP not mentioned in any vendor documentation reviewed.

name: Databricks Genie Code
model_backend: Azure OpenAI / OpenAI (via "partner-powered AI features") and Anthropic (Claude) on Databricks; falls back to Databricks-hosted open-source models (e.g. OpenAI GPT OSS variants) when partner-powered features are disabled by admin. Backend is Databricks-managed; users cannot swap models themselves.
data_handling_note: Databricks explicitly states it does not train foundation models on customer prompts or responses, and partner model providers use zero-data-retention endpoints (no retention even for abuse monitoring). This applies by default to all paid workspace tiers - no special tier needed to unlock no-train. However, Genie Code runs fully in Databricks' cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP); there is no self-hosted or air-gapped deployment of Genie Code itself. Data residency is managed via Databricks Geos (Designated Services). Regulated workloads (PHI, PCI, FedRAMP, IRAP) require enabling the Compliance Security Profile in workspace settings. The default free monthly allowance (150 DBUs/user) carries the same no-train guarantee.

suitability_notes.personal: No-train + zero-retention is the default, and Databricks has GDPR/ISO 27701 coverage, but data is processed in Databricks cloud. A DPA must be in place (contact Databricks account team). EU data residency requires selecting an EU geo region. For pseudonymised research data under GDPR, this is workable with a signed DPA and EU geo; cfg reflects the need for those contractual/config steps.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

compliance: FedRAMP High (AWS GovCloud; Azure Commercial expected summer 2026)
pricing: Free monthly allowance: 150 DBUs per user per month (approx. $10.50 in US East), covering Genie LLM usage across Genie, Genie Spaces, and Genie Code. Pay-as-you-go beyond that at ~$0.07/DBU (varies by cloud/region), effective July 6, 2026. Genie compute (SQL Serverless, etc.) billed separately. Service principals pay for all LLM usage with no free allowance. Enterprise pricing negotiated via account team.
academic: No dedicated academic discount program documented publicly. Databricks University Alliance exists for institutions and research groups, but academic pricing requires direct engagement with the Databricks sales team. A free $400 trial credit is available for new accounts.
use_cases: ML model training, experiment tracking (MLflow integration), and model deployment
SQL query building and optimization in SQL Editor
Notebook-based data science and EDA automation
Agentic debugging of pipeline failures and errors
Scheduled autonomous data operations (overnight jobs, pipeline summarization)
notes: Genie Code is an agentic AI coding assistant embedded in Databricks notebooks, SQL Editor, Lakeflow Pipelines Editor, AI/BI dashboards, and MLflow. It replaced Databricks Assistant in March 2026. Agent mode allows autonomous multi-step task execution with user-confirmable code execution. Governed by Unity Catalog permissions - it can only access data the user is authorized for. MCP server support added (connect external tools). Quotient AI acquired simultaneously to embed continuous RL-based evaluation. As of June 2026, used by 90% of Databricks customers. Genie products grew 10x over the prior year. A separate product (Genie One) was announced in June 2026 as an agentic coworker for non-technical users, distinct from Genie Code which targets data engineers and data scientists.

name: Posit AI / Posit Assistant
model_backend: Anthropic Claude (sole provider for managed Posit AI subscription; Positron and Posit Workbench also support BYOK with AWS Bedrock, Azure Foundry, Google Vertex, OpenAI, GitHub Copilot, custom providers)
data_handling_note: Zero Data Retention (ZDR) agreement with Anthropic is in effect for all Posit AI subscribers: prompts and outputs are processed in real-time and immediately discarded, never stored or used for model training. The DEFAULT is opt-OUT of trace data sharing (only basic operational metadata kept 30 days). Users who opt IN allow Posit to retain trace data up to 5 years for improvement. Posit itself does not store prompts or code. On Posit Workbench (enterprise self-hosted), BYOK enables routing traffic through an organization's own cloud boundary (Azure Foundry, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex, etc.), giving stricter data governance. No EU data residency option noted on the managed cloud tier.

suitability_notes.personal: The default managed Posit AI cloud tier uses ZDR with Anthropic (data not retained or trained on) and Posit does not store prompts. This makes it usable for pseudonymised/GDPR-adjacent data with care, but there is no explicit DPA or EU data residency for the cloud tier, and data transits to US servers. For formal GDPR compliance, the enterprise BYOK path via Posit Workbench (self-hosted) with an approved EU-cloud provider is the safer route.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

compliance: GDPR (EU-US Data Privacy Framework certified by Posit)
No SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / HIPAA BAA documented for managed cloud tier
pricing: $20/month per user (no free tier; free trial with credits available, no credit card required). Includes ~$15 API credits for Posit Assistant chat (Claude Haiku 4.5 / higher models) plus unlimited Next Edit Suggestions. Optional auto top-up for heavy users. No academic/student discount currently; Posit states they are 'actively exploring' academic pricing. RStudio and Positron IDEs themselves remain free and open-source. Posit Workbench (enterprise self-hosted) has separate licensing; BYOK path for AI is included in Workbench but enterprise AI pricing not published.
academic: No academic discount exists for Posit AI as of June 2026. Posit acknowledges cost barriers and is exploring options but has not launched a program. Posit Cloud (separate product) has a free educational tier (25 project hours/month) but that does not include Posit AI. Posit Workbench has academic/research server pricing available via sales.
use_cases: R and Python code writing and debugging in RStudio and Positron
Exploratory data analysis with live session context (DataFrames, variables, plots)
Multi-step agentic tasks via Plan Mode
Console auditing and reproducible research workflows (Workbench enterprise)
notes: Posit AI (the managed $20/month subscription) is distinct from the Positron IDE's open BYOK assistant (free, user supplies own API keys). The lead URL posit.co/products/ai correctly points to the managed subscription. The 'posit.ai' domain exists but appears to be a minimal landing page. Posit Workbench BYOK is the enterprise self-hosted path for regulated-data environments. The ZDR agreement is Posit-level (covering all paying subscribers), not a negotiated enterprise add-on - this is a meaningful distinction versus competitors. No EU data residency is available on the managed cloud tier as of June 2026.

name: marimo
deployment: local-capable / self-hostable / cloud (molab, free beta on CoreWeave)
model_backend: BYO key: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, AWS Bedrock, Ollama, LM Studio, GitHub Copilot, any OpenAI-compatible provider; no built-in hosted model
data_handling_note: The core marimo package runs entirely locally (pip install, no cloud account required); no data leaves the machine unless the user configures an AI backend that calls an external API, or uploads notebooks to molab (the cloud-hosted service). For the local install with Ollama/LM Studio, all computation and AI inference stays on-device. molab (free cloud beta) stores notebooks in Cloudflare R2 and runs on CoreWeave; the terms state marimo acts as a CCPA "service provider," does not sell or use data for other commercial purposes, and does not train on user notebooks, but molab is NOT suitable for sensitive research data because notebooks are public by default (undiscoverable but publicly accessible) and no HIPAA BAA or SOC 2 certification has been disclosed. No EU data residency guarantee for molab.

suitability_notes.personal: Local install with Ollama or no AI features: data never leaves machine, fully suitable. If using molab cloud or an external AI API (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) the notebook contents and variable context are sent to the configured provider; suitability then depends on that provider's data handling. Configure local-only mode to unlock safe use with personal data.
suitability_notes.special: Self-hosted or local install only, with all AI completions routed to local models (Ollama/LM Studio). molab explicitly prohibits uploading protected health information in its terms. No SOC 2 or HIPAA BAA has been announced; until such certifications exist, cloud molab is unsuitable for special-category data. Local deployment on own infrastructure is the compliant path.

compliance: GDPR (terms reference EU Regulation 2016/679; CCPA service-provider status)
pricing: Free and open-source (Apache-2.0). molab cloud is free during beta (4 CPU / 32 GB RAM; optional NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPU; 12-hour sessions; limited persistent storage). No paid tiers announced as of June 2026; marimo cloud was listed as "closed beta" on the cloud subdomain. Enterprise/commercial arrangements via demo request.
academic: No dedicated academic pricing; core tool is fully open-source and free. marimo has run notebook competitions for researchers (molab Notebook Competition). Trusted by Stanford, UC Berkeley, Johns Hopkins, SLAC - no special license needed.
use_cases: Reactive Python notebook with auto-rerunning cells on dependency change
SQL querying of dataframes (Polars, Pandas, DuckDB, SQLite, Postgres, MySQL)
Interactive data exploration with sliders, plots, tables (no callbacks needed)
Deployment of notebooks as web apps or WebAssembly HTML files
Git-friendly reproducible research (stored as pure .py files)
AI-assisted coding with BYO model key (local or cloud)
Agent-driven notebook sessions via Agent Client Protocol (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode)
notes: Privacy policy page (marimo.io/pages/legal/privacy) redirects to CoreWeave's privacy policy, suggesting molab infrastructure is fully on CoreWeave. The terms page (marimo.io/pages/legal/terms) confirms CCPA service-provider status and no secondary commercial use of data, but no SOC 2 / HIPAA / ISO 27001 certifications are disclosed anywhere in current public documentation. Telemetry settings for the local editor were not found in public docs; no evidence of phone-home analytics in the local install. molab notebooks are public-but-undiscoverable by default (like unlisted GitHub gists) - a known security concern flagged by the community (robots.txt crawling issue, July 2025). For ELIXIR-CZ research: local install with Ollama backend is the recommended privacy-safe configuration for personal or special-category data.

name: Runcell
model_backend: GPT (OpenAI), Claude (Anthropic), Gemini (Google) - accessed via Runcell subscription, no BYO key required; higher tiers unlock access to more advanced models
data_handling_note: Paid plans (Pro/Pro+/Team) enable Privacy Mode by default: prompts, context, and model outputs are not used to train or fine-tune models. The free/Hobby tier does NOT have Privacy Mode - data may be retained and used to improve Runcell. Raw notebook/file contents stay on the user's machine; only prompts and relevant code snippets are sent to cloud LLM APIs. The vendor instructs third-party LLM providers not to train on user data where available. No SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA-BAA, or GDPR compliance certifications are documented publicly. No self-hosting or on-prem option is publicly available (enterprise contact suggested).

suitability_notes.personal: Personal/pseudonymised data requires a paid plan (Pro or higher) to activate Privacy Mode, which disables training on user inputs. Even then, code snippets and prompts are transmitted to cloud LLM APIs, and no DPA or GDPR compliance documentation is publicly available. Use only on paid tier with careful data minimisation; cfg = must upgrade to paid plan.
suitability_notes.special: No self-hosting, air-gapped, or on-prem deployment is available. Special-category health/genetic/clinical data should not be sent to this cloud service - no BAA, no on-prem, no documented GDPR-compliant processing agreement.

pricing: Free/Hobby: 50 credits/month (plus 50 bonus credits during beta). Pro: $20/month (500 credits/month, unlimited code completions, priority support). Pro+: $60/month. Team: $40/user/month. Credits consumed by Ask/Chat and Agent features; code completions unlimited on paid plans.
academic: No dedicated academic or student discount plan found. Tool is used in academic data science teaching contexts but offers no institutional pricing or academic tier as of June 2026.
use_cases: Autonomous multi-step data analysis in JupyterLab
Cross-session project memory for iterative research workflows
Output-aware reasoning (reads charts, plots, DataFrame previews)
Automated cell execution, debugging, and error recovery
Life-science ML and risk modelling workflows
Interactive learning and algorithm explanation in notebooks
EDA, data cleaning, visualisation, and feature engineering automation
notes: Runcell is a JupyterLab extension + Python package (pip install runcell; requires Python 3.10+, JupyterLab 4.4+). Also supports VS Code Notebooks and Classic Notebook. Classified as type=cloud because its core value is the cloud-connected autonomous agent - raw notebooks remain local but all reasoning/inference is routed through cloud LLM APIs. The privacy policy explicitly states free plan data may be used for model improvement. No DPA template, GDPR Article 28 processor agreement, or compliance certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA) were found in public documentation. Enterprise/custom deployment enquiries directed to vendor contact. Confidence is medium: the pricing page did not fully render and some plan details (Pro+, Team price) were sourced from third-party listings; Privacy Mode guarantee is vendor-stated, not audited.

name: JetBrains Datalore
model_backend: JetBrains AI (default); BYOK: OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, or OpenAI-compatible self-hosted models (on-prem, from 2026.1)
data_handling_note: JetBrains AI Terms explicitly commit to not training on user inputs/outputs/code by any provider. For Cloud paid tier, detailed code-related data requires explicit opt-in consent from organization admins (disabled by default for commercial users). The Cloud Free tier collects analytics by default on legitimate interest with opt-out available. On-Premises deployment: JetBrains states "We do not see or have access to Your Data," giving zero data egress by design. DPA (GDPR) is available for all customers. Default Cloud Free tier carries higher risk; Cloud paid tier is safer with org-level consent controls; On-Premises is the gold standard for data governance.

suitability_notes.personal: On Cloud paid tier, ensure organizational admin has not opted in to detailed code data collection; DPA must be signed. On-Premises deployment avoids data egress entirely and is the recommended path for GDPR-sensitive personal data. Cloud Free is not suitable due to default analytics collection.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-first: the AI runs in the vendor cloud by default (zdr). Only an enterprise on-prem / self-hosted deployment on infrastructure you control could suit special-category data — and only with DPO/IRB approval; the default cloud product is not suitable.

compliance: GDPR (DPA available for all customers, Czech Republic controller, SCCs for international transfers)
Data residency: EU default for cloud; on-prem removes cloud dependency entirely
pricing: Cloud Free: free (120 CPU S hours/month, 10 GB storage, 2 parallel notebooks, 1 workspace). Cloud paid: $35/user/month ($420/user/year; 750 CPU L hours/month, 20 GB, unlimited parallel notebooks, Datalore AI, team management). On-Premises: $1,800/user/year (self-hosted or JetBrains-managed dedicated instance, all features unlimited). 14-day free trial available. No dedicated academic discount for Datalore (excluded from JetBrains educational IDE license program).
academic: No dedicated academic pricing; Datalore is classified as a "Team Tool" and excluded from JetBrains' free student/teacher IDE pack. Students and researchers must use the Cloud Free tier or purchase paid plans. On-Premises pricing ($1,800/user/year) may be negotiated for institutions.
use_cases: Jupyter-compatible Python/R/SQL notebooks for data science and research
Collaborative real-time notebook editing for teams
AI-assisted code generation, error fixing, and explanation in notebooks
No-code DataFrame exploration via Data Explorer Cells (2026.1)
Interactive data reports and dashboards shareable without code exposure
On-premises deployment for regulated data environments with BYOK AI
notes: BYOK in 2026.1 is specifically for On-Premises deployments (instance-wide admin configuration); Cloud users use JetBrains AI by default. ISO 27001 certification not found in public documentation - only SOC 2 is confirmed. HIPAA BAA not publicly documented. The JetBrains DPA is available to all paying customers without requiring a specific enterprise tier. "runs_locally" is false because Datalore is a cloud/server product; On-Premises still requires a server infrastructure, not a local laptop install.

name: ChatGPT Advanced Data Analysis
model_backend: GPT-5 / GPT-5.5 (flagship as of 2026; previously GPT-4o). Feature is built into the ChatGPT interface - not BYO-key.
data_handling_note: No-train is the default ONLY for ChatGPT Team, Business, Enterprise (and the API). For Free, Go, Plus, and Pro personal accounts the "Improve the model for everyone" toggle is ON by default - users must manually disable it in Settings -> Data Controls. Even with opt-out on personal tiers, there is no DPA and no contractual guarantee. Team/Business/Enterprise include a DPA, and Enterprise + Edu can add EU data residency (configurable, not default) and configure zero-data retention. A HIPAA BAA is available only for sales-managed Enterprise/Edu and the ChatGPT for Healthcare product. EU data residency available for Enterprise/Edu since early 2025; in-region inference in Europe from January 2026.

suitability_notes.personal: Personal/pseudonymised (GDPR) data requires at minimum ChatGPT Team or Business tier with a signed DPA and training disabled by default. On Free/Plus/Pro the default trains on data and there is no DPA - not suitable. With Enterprise + signed DPA + EU data residency configured: usable for GDPR personal data.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-only. ZDR / HIPAA-BAA is a contractual assurance, but data still leaves your environment — not appropriate for identifiable or controlled-access (DUA) health/genetic data. Use a tool that runs on infrastructure you control, and confirm with your DPO / IRB.

pricing: Free ($0/mo, limited); Go ($8/mo); Plus ($20/mo); Pro $100 ($100/mo); Pro $200 ($200/mo); Business ($25/user/mo annual or $30/mo); Enterprise (custom, ~$40-75/seat/mo, ~150-seat minimum). No current official student discount (a 2-month free Plus promotion for US/Canada students ran March-May 2025 and has ended). ChatGPT Edu is available for universities via campus licensing.
academic: No standing academic or student discount as of June 2026. A limited 2-month free Plus promotion for US/Canada university students ran in early 2025 but ended May 31, 2025. Universities can negotiate ChatGPT Edu campus licenses. Individual researchers pay the same rates as all users.
use_cases: Exploratory data analysis (EDA) on CSV/Excel/PDF uploads
Automated Python code generation and execution in a sandboxed environment
Data visualisation (bar, line, pie, scatter; interactive and downloadable charts)
File import from Google Drive and Microsoft OneDrive
notes: The tool is purely cloud-based with no self-hosted or local option, making it unsuitable for air-gapped research environments. The Python sandbox runs server-side on OpenAI infrastructure. Results (code, charts, processed files) can be downloaded. The "Advanced Data Analysis" brand name is largely legacy - current UI simply calls it "data analysis" or the "Data Analyst" custom GPT. Researchers on personal plans (including Plus) should explicitly opt out of model training before uploading any research data. For ELIXIR-CZ use, Team-tier minimum is recommended for any GDPR-scoped data; Enterprise with BAA is required for any clinical/health/genetic data.
current_name: ChatGPT Data Analysis (also surfaced as the "Data Analyst" GPT)

name: Qodo
model_backend: Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, DeepSeek, NVIDIA models (BYO key); local models via Ollama also supported in self-hosted PR-Agent
data_handling_note: Paid subscribers (Teams and Enterprise): no training on customer code, explicit contractual prohibition in Business ToS. Data deleted from all Qodo storage within 48 hours (troubleshooting window only); immediate deletion on request. Third-party LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI) are also contractually bound to zero data retention for paid Qodo users. Free/trial tier: Qodo does use data to improve models (opt-out available in account portal). Enterprise tier additionally offers single-tenant SaaS, on-premises (Kubernetes-based), and air-gapped deployment - in those cases data never leaves customer infrastructure. EU data residency available: GCP (US or EU) and AWS (US, EU, or Australia) at customer's choice. DPA available with SCCs for EEA/UK transfers. Default cloud free tier is risky for any sensitive research data.

suitability_notes.personal: Requires paid tier (Teams or Enterprise) + signed DPA to avoid training and ensure 48-hour deletion; EU data residency selectable on Teams/Enterprise. Free tier must not be used for personal data.
suitability_notes.special: Cloud-first: the AI runs in the vendor cloud by default (no-train). Only an enterprise on-prem / self-hosted deployment on infrastructure you control could suit special-category data — and only with DPO/IRB approval; the default cloud product is not suitable.

pricing: Free 14-day trial (no CC required). Teams: $30/user/month (credit-based, ~$0.012/credit; up to 30 users). Enterprise: custom pricing, annual contract, for 30+ users. Open-source PR-Agent: free, self-hostable.
academic: No dedicated academic or student discount found on the public pricing page. Free tier for qualified open-source projects via Qodo for Open Source program. Researchers on open-source projects may qualify; otherwise Teams pricing applies.
use_cases: AI-powered pull request code review (automated, agentic)
IDE-integrated real-time code review (VS Code, JetBrains)
Test generation and code quality enforcement
Self-hosted / air-gapped code review via PR-Agent open-source
notes: The open-source PR-Agent (Apache 2.0 licensed, github.com/qodo-ai/pr-agent) is the fully free self-hosted path: deploy via Docker, bring your own LLM keys or point at a local Ollama instance, zero data egress. This is the lowest-risk option for ELIXIR researchers handling sensitive code. The commercial Qodo platform (IDE plugin + cloud PR review) requires Teams or Enterprise tier for privacy-safe use with GDPR-scoped data. Air-gapped on-prem (Kubernetes) is an Enterprise feature and requires contacting Qodo sales. No HIPAA-BAA or ISO 27001 certification was found in public documentation - researchers working with clinical or health data should confirm these with Qodo before procurement.

name: Ollama
model_backend: Open-weight models run locally (Llama 4, Qwen Coder, DeepSeek Coder, Granite, Mistral, Gemma, GLM, MiniMax, Kimi-K2, NVIDIA Nemotron, and 100+ others pulled from the Ollama registry); Ollama Cloud (optional) hosts these same models on Ollama-managed GPU infrastructure
data_handling_note: Local install (all tiers): prompts, responses, and model interactions never leave the user's machine - Ollama explicitly states it does not collect, store, transmit, or have access to locally processed content. No training on user data at any tier. Cloud models (Free/Pro/Max): content is processed transiently with technical measures to minimise retention and is never used for training; no persistent storage beyond request fulfilment. No SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA, or formal DPA is publicly documented - for regulated PHI/clinical data, the fully local/air-gapped path is the only validated route. Default Free tier with local-only use is safe; cloud tiers carry standard cloud-service caveats.

suitability_notes.personal: Personal/pseudonymised data is safe when Ollama runs locally (default install): no data ever leaves the machine, no vendor processing, no training. Avoid routing personal data through Ollama Cloud tiers unless a DPA is in place (none currently published).
suitability_notes.special: Special-category data (health, genetic, clinical) is suitable only when Ollama runs fully on-premises or air-gapped - the data never leaves the controlled environment and no third-party processor is involved. The cloud tiers (Free/Pro/Max) must not be used for special-category data: no HIPAA BAA, no formal DPA, and no SOC 2 are currently documented. Run locally to unlock this use case.

compliance: GDPR (local run: no processor involved; cloud: transient processing, no training, GDPR rights acknowledged but no formal DPA published)
No HIPAA BAA (local air-gap is the HIPAA path)
pricing: Free: $0 - unlimited local models, 1 concurrent cloud model, light cloud usage; Pro: $20/month or $200/year - 50x more cloud usage, 3 concurrent cloud models, private model sharing; Max: $100/month - 5x more than Pro, 10 concurrent cloud models, continuous agent tasks; Team: coming soon (SSO, MDM, centralised billing). Local execution is unlimited and free across all tiers.
academic: No dedicated academic or research discount is publicly listed. The core runtime (local) is MIT-licensed and free; researchers can self-host at zero cost with no usage caps.
use_cases: Local/air-gapped LLM inference for privacy-sensitive research
Code completion and generation via Continue.dev, GitHub Copilot (local agent mode), JetBrains AI, Sourcegraph Cody, Qudo
Bioinformatics and scientific data analysis with open-weight models on-prem
Enterprise GDPR/HIPAA-governed AI workflows without cloud data egress
Rapid prototyping and model evaluation (100+ models available via one-command pull)
Agentic workflows and multi-model pipelines on private infrastructure
notes: GitHub Copilot agent mode integration with local Ollama launched March 2026. Ollama 0.30 (June 2026) added MLX engine support for Apple Silicon and broader GGUF hardware compatibility. The Team tier (SSO, MDM installers) is listed as coming soon as of June 2026. No enterprise DPA or BAA contact process is documented on the public site - researchers needing formal agreements should contact hello@ollama.com directly.
